㐫.e: A container 凵 of wickedness 乂

㐮.e: Using two hands 口 to help someone remove their clothes 衣

㐯.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *loŋ) and ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): abbreviated semantic 庚 + phonetic 同 (*lˤoŋ).

㐷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mraːs): semantic 亻 (“person”) + phonetic 馬 (OC *mraːʔ).

㐸.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㐸 – see 欠 (“to lack; to be deficient; to owe; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 欠).

㑁.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 亻 (“person”) + phonetic 出 (OC *kʰljuds, *kʰljud).

㑄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 人 (“person”) + phonetic 每 (OC *mɯːʔ).

㑇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zrus, *ʔsro, *rnuːb): semantic 人 (“person”) + phonetic 芻 (OC *sʰro).

㑉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 亻 + phonetic 夙 (OC *suɡ)

㑌.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㑌 – see 尪 (“disabled person”).
(This character is a variant form of 尪).

㑚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 亻 + phonetic 那 (OC *naːl, *naːlʔ, *naːls).

㑢.e:
; Etymology 1
; Etymology 2
From 佢 (“he; she; it”), with -k (plural marker) and tone change (Gan, 1997). This may be a contraction of 佢個 /佢个 (Zhang, 1997) or 佢屋 (Zhang, 2005; Kwok, 2006).
Compare 偌 (“you (plural)”), from 你 (“you (singular)”).

㑳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zrus, *ʔsro, *rnuːb): semantic 人 (“person”) + phonetic 芻 (OC *sʰro).

㒃.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㒃 – see 貳 (“secondary; assistant; to be unfaithful; to be disloyal; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 貳).

㒍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 絫 (OC *rolʔ).

㒜.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 亻 + phonetic 與 (OC *la, *laʔ, *las).

㒧.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㒧 – see 儷 (“married couple; of a couplet; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 儷).

㒰.e: Jade 玉 put away 入 for safe-keeping

㒷.e: Depicts four hands (舁) working together (同) to raise something.

㒼.e: To weights 入 balanced on a scale 巾

㓠.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㓠 – see 玷 (“flaw in gem; defect in jade; flaw in character; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 玷).

㓢.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 各 (OC *klaːɡ) + semantic 刀 (“knife”).

㓣.e: Found in the historical Yupian 《玉篇 (Yùpiān)》 dictionary from the Liang dynasty (543 AD).

㓨.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰeɡs): phonetic 夾 (OC *kreːb) + semantic 刂 (“knift”).

㓪.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㓪 – see 朗 (“clear; bright; loud and clear; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 朗).

㓸.e: To chop 斤 a tree trunk

㔀.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 景 (OC *kraŋʔ) + semantic 刂 (“knife”).

㔄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 粟 (OC *soɡ) + semantic 刀 (“knife”).

㔅.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 肅 (OC *sɯwɢ) + semantic 刂 (“knife”).

㔹.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 十 (“ten”) + phonetic 力 (OC *rɯɡ).
力 (“power; strength”) also acts as a semantic component.

㔾.e: A rolled-up scroll or kneeling person; compare 卩

㕋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 厂 (“cliff”) + phonetic 金 (OC *krɯm).

㕒.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 厂 + phonetic 義 (OC *ŋrals).

㕙.e: From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *b-jəw-n ~ bwəj (“rat, bamboo rat, rabbit, hare”) (STEDT; Schuessler, 2007).

㕟.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㕟 – see 喟 (“to heave a sigh; to sigh”).
(This character is a variant form of 喟).

㕡.e: Water flowing through a gorge 谷

㕣.e: The bottom part is a mouth 口, while the top component 八 is either the representation of an open space between two things (e.g., mountain walls) or, less likely, the representation of stagnant water. Used as the phonetic component of 鉛. See also 谷. Unrelated to the top component of 兑.

㕭.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 + phonetic 夭 (OC *qoːwʔ, *qrow, *qrowʔ).
Probably the same word as 咬 (MC 'aew).

㕰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 + phonetic 毛 (OC *maːw, *maːws).

㕳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 + phonetic 凶 (OC *qʰoŋ).

㕶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 + phonetic 五 (OC *ŋaːʔ).

㕷.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㕷 – see 咟 (“to call; to yell; anxious; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 咟).

㕸.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 + phonetic 立 (OC *rɯb).

㖃.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㖃 – see 吼 (“to roar; to shout; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 吼).

㖕.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㖕 – see 𠱫.
(This character is a variant form of 𠱫).

㖧.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㖧 – see 吻 (“proboscis; lip; corner of the lips; to shut the lips; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 吻).

㖭.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㖭 – see 舔 (“to lick; to suck up to; to brownnose”).
(This character is a variant form of 舔).

㖸.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㖸 – see 噱.
(This character is a variant form of 噱).

㖽.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 + phonetic 頁 (OC *leb, *ɡleːd).

㗂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 省 (OC *sreŋʔ, *sleŋʔ).

㗎.e:
There are two theories:
* From the common Japanese particle か (ka).
* From the second syllable of Japanese 馬鹿(ばか) (baka, “fool; idiot”), used by Japanese soldiers during the Sino–Japanese War.

㘎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 闞 (OC *kʰlaːms, *qʰreːmʔ, *hŋraːms).

㘚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 闞 (OC *kʰlaːms, *qʰreːmʔ, *hŋraːms).

㘧.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 予 (OC *la, *laʔ).

㘨.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 內 (OC *nuːbs).

㘩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 比 (OC *piʔ, *pis, *bi, *bis, *biɡ).

㙦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 斜 (OC *lja, *laː) + semantic 土 (“earth; soil”).

㙳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 + phonetic 感 (OC *kɯːmʔ).

㚘.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): duplication of 夫

㛠.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 𡢃 – see 嫻 (“elegant, gentle and quiet; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 嫻).
From 囝 /囡 (kán, “son; child”) as in Quanzhou Hokkien 囝仔 /囡仔 (kán-á, “child”) as per Douglas (1873).

㛲.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㛲 – see 髮 (“the hair on the human head”).
(This character is a variant form of 髮).

㜂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 封 (OC *poŋ, *poŋs) + semantic 女 (“woman”).

㜅.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 (“woman”) + phonetic 畜 (OC *l̥ʰuɡs, *l̥ʰuɡ, *hluɡ).

㜆.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 (“woman”) + phonetic 迷.

㜇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 (“woman”) + phonetic 恚 (OC *qʷes).

㜈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 務 (OC *moɡs) + semantic 女 (“woman”).

㜉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 (“woman”) + phonetic 邕 (OC *qoŋ).

㜊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 (“woman”) + phonetic 展 (OC *tenʔ, *tens).

㜋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 (“woman”) + phonetic 益 (OC *qleɡ).

㜍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 (“woman”) + phonetic 唐 (OC *ɡl'aːŋ).

㜎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 (“woman”) + phonetic 奚 (OC *ɡeː).

㜛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 女 + phonetic 軟 (OC *njonʔ).

㜟.e: Likely a corrupted form of 娵 (jū).

㜺.e: Northern Wu sense spread via Shanghainese and originated from Ningbonese.

㝀.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 母 (“mother”) + 子 (“child”). 母 (OC *mɯʔ, “mother”) is graphically cognate to 女 (OC *naʔ, *nas, “woman”). See 好 for more information.

㝌.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㝌 – see 疚 (“chronic disease; chronic illness; guilt; sorrow; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 疚).

㝒.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㝒 – see 居 (“to live; to dwell; to reside; to occupy; to be located at; to rank; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 居).

㝛.e: A person 亻 sleeping in a bed 百 under a roof 宀

㝦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsuns): semantic 宀 (“roof”) + phonetic 雋 (OC *zonʔ).

㝵.e: A hand 寸 grabbing a shell 旦

㞎.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 尸 (“body”) + phonetic 巴.
The choosing of 尸 (shī) radical is seemingly influenced by character 屎 (“faeces”).

㞗.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 尸 + phonetic 求 (OC *ɡu).

㞞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 尸 (“body”) + phonetic 從.

㞟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 奠 (OC *diːŋs) + semantic 尸.

㟂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 山 (“mountain”) + phonetic 母 (OC *mɯʔ).

㟌.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 山 + phonetic 尨 (OC *mroːŋ).

㟖.e: Borrowed from Zhuang rungh (“level land between mountains”).

㠯.e:
Uncertain.
* Shuowen Jiezi: 巳 (sì) turned.
* Alternatively: Pictogram (象形) of a plow - original form of 耜 (OC *ljɯʔ).

㡭.e: An unbroken thread 纟 from the top to the bottom

㡿.e: Archaic variant of 斥. Composed of 广 (roof) and 屰 (contrary; oppose).

㢆.e: A house 广 in a village 里

㢟.e: A foot 止 strolling down the road 廴

㢴.e: A bird settling into its nest, representing sunset; compare 東

㣎.e: Seeds 彡 falling off a ripe 白 plant 小

㣼.e: A knife 刃 piercing the heart 心; 刃 also provides the pronunciation

㥪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *roː, *ro, *roʔ): semantic 心 (“heart”) + phonetic 婁 (OC *ɡ·roː, *ɡ·ro).

㦂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声) and ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): phonetic 常 (OC *djaŋ) + semantic 心 (“heart”).

㦴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 戈 (“halberd”) + phonetic 各 (OC *klaːɡ).

㧄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 手 + phonetic 凵 (OC *kʰomʔ, *kʰomʔ).

㧅.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㧅 – see 朷.
(This character is a variant form of 朷).

㧏.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *klaːŋ, *klaːŋs): semantic 手 (“hand”) + phonetic 岡 (OC *klaːŋ)

㧐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *soŋʔ): semantic 手 + phonetic 雙 (OC *sroːŋ).

㧜.e: To surround 匝 something with a hand 扌

㧦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 扌 (“hand”) + phonetic 旬 (OC *sɢʷin).

㧬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 𢀜 (gǒng) + semantic 手 (“hand”).

㧰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rawɢ, *reːwɢ): semantic 手 (“hand”) + phonetic 樂 (OC *ŋraːwɢs, *raːwɢ, *ŋraːwɢ)

㨖.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 手 (OC *hnjɯwʔ) + semantic 致

㨘.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㨘 – see 擤 (“to blow one's nose”).
(This character is a variant form of 擤).

㨛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 扌 (“hand”) + phonetic 閃 (OC *hljamʔ, *hljams).

㨰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 手 + phonetic 衮 (OC *kluːnʔ).

㨳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 扌 (“hand”) + phonetic 寄 (OC *krals).

㨴.e: Has a nasal-stop alternant in 揭 (kit3). Compare Wuming Zhuang gwenj (“to uncover”).

㨾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 扌 (“hand”) + phonetic 羕 (OC *laŋs).

㩄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 扌 (“hand”) + phonetic 崽 (OC *ʔslɯːʔ, *srɯː, *srɯː).

㩒.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㩒 – see 擒 (“to catch; to capture; to seize; to arrest; to hold; to grasp; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 擒).

㩡.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 手 (“hand”) + phonetic 磊 (OC *ruːlʔ).

㩧.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 扌 (“hand”) + phonetic 暴 (OC *boːwɢs, *boːɡ).

㩲.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 扌 (“hand”) + phonetic 雚 (OC *koːns).

㩳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *soŋʔ): semantic 手 + phonetic 雙 (OC *sroːŋ).

㩻.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 危 (OC *ŋrol) + semantic 支.

㩿.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 乞 (OC *kʰɯds, *kʰɯd) + semantic 攴.

㪅.e: Phonosemantic compound. 攴 represents the meaning and 丙 represents the sound.

㪠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 兼 + semantic 攴.

㪣.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㪣 – see 敲 (“to hit; to strike; to tap; to rap; to knock; to rip off; to overcharge; to blackmail; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 敲).

㫰.e:
Possibly from etymology 1.
Alternatively, possibly from alternative MC Reading of 晾 having went through voiced shang merger into qu. However, most Wu cognates demonstrate voiced qu tone, even in Southern Wu varieties that do not observed voiced shang merger into qu.
Compare Cantonese 晾 (long6).

㫻.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 日 + phonetic 侖 (OC *run).

㬅.e: Two hands 日, 又 pulling an eye open 罒

㬈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 日 (“sun”) + phonetic 𥁕 (OC *quːn).

㬊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 日 (“sun”) + phonetic 爰 (OC *ɢʷan)

㬎.e: Tiny 幺 motes of dust 灬 in a sunbeam 日

㬟.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 勿 + 曾

㬱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰlɯːmʔ, *zlam): phonetic 兓 (OC *sl'ɯm) + semantic 口. The semantic component was originally 口 in oracle bone script, which later corrupted into 甘 in bronze script, then 曰 in seal script, and finally 日 in standard script.

㬳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 月 (“moon”) + phonetic 午 (OC *ŋaːʔ)

㬹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 足 (“foot”) + phonetic 爭.

㭎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 岡 (OC *klaːŋ).

㭬.e: Unorthodox variant simplified from 椓 (豖 → 豕) found in the Qing dynasty dictionary Zhengzitong (正字通 (Zhèngzìtōng)) dictionary.

㮠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 + phonetic 榮 (OC *ɢʷreŋ)

㮣.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㮣 – see 概 (“general idea; general; rough; approximate; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 概).

㯃.e: The sap 氵 of the varnish tree 桼; 桼 also provides the pronunciation

㯄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 林 (“forest”) + phonetic 巵.

㱖.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 止 + phonetic 卒 (OC *ʔsuːd, *sʰuːd, *ʔsud).

㱩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'oːɡ): semantic 歹 (“bad”) + phonetic 𧶠 (OC *luɡ).

㲋.e: A rabbit looking to the left

㲞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 卒 (OC *ʔsuːd, *sʰuːd, *ʔsud) + semantic 毛 (“fur, hair”).

㲲.e: Borrowed from a language spoken in the ancient Gaochang kingdom; compare Modern Persian دیبا (dibâ, “brocade”).

㲾.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 宀 + 水.

㳡.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kloːl, *qloːl): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 過 (OC *kloːl, *kloːls).

㳽.e: Water 氵 everywhere 彌; 彌 also provides the pronunciation

㴋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sɯːw, *sɯwɢ): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 肅 (OC *sɯwɢ).

㴑.e: To seek the source 朔 of a river 氵

㴽.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 氵 (“water”) + phonetic 悉 (OC *sid).

㵃.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 氵 (“river; water”) + phonetic 蛇 (OC *l̥ʰaːl, *ɦljaːl, *lal).

㵶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 氵 + phonetic 截 (OC *zeːd).

㶉.e: Water 氵 fowl 鷄; 鷄 also provides the pronunciation

㶠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 水 + phonetic 驛 (OC *laːɡ, “water”).

㷙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 火 + phonetic 前 (OC *zleːn).

㷥.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 炎 + phonetic 丞 (OC *ɡljɯŋ, *ɡljɯŋs).

㷫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 殸 (OC *kʰreːŋ, *kʰeːŋs) + semantic 火.

㷱.e: Calligraphic variant. Simplified from 熊 (能 → 䏻).

㷳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 雁 (OC *ŋraːns) + semantic 火 (“fire”).

㷻.e: A reckless 胡 flame 火; 胡 also provides the pronunciation

㸪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 牛 (“ox; cattle”) + phonetic 川 (OC *kʰjon).

㹊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 牛 (“cattle”) + phonetic 隺.

㹙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 牛 (“ox”) + phonetic 䁝 (OC *qʷraːŋʔ).

㹢.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㹢 – see 𤠙.
(This character is a variant form of 𤠙).

㺋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犭 (“dog”) + phonetic 翁 (OC *qloːŋ).

㺑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 參 (OC *sʰluːm, *sʰluːms, *sloːm, *sʰrum, *srum).

㺔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犭 + phonetic 爲 (OC *ɢʷal, *ɢʷal, *ɢʷals).

㺦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犭 (“dog”) + phonetic 聯 (OC *ɡ·ron).

㻅.e:
Characters in the same phonetic series (會) (Zhengzhang, 2003)
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰroːbs): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 會 (OC *koːbs, *ɡoːbs).

㻇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 𤣩 + phonetic 全 (OC *zlon).

㼷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 耑 (OC *toːn) + semantic 瓦.

㽐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 甘 + phonetic 兼 (OC *keːm, *keːms).

㽕.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 由 (OC *lɯw) + semantic 𢎘.
Same word as 悠 (OC *lɯw, “drawn out; faraway; distant”) (Schuessler, 2007).

㽣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 田 + phonetic 或 (OC *ɡʷɯːɡ).

㾌.e: Unorthodox variant of 癬. Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 + phonetic 先.

㾙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l̥ʰɯl, *hrɯnʔ, *hlɯns): semantic 肉 (“flesh”) + phonetic 希 (OC *hlɯl)

㾣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“sickness”) + phonetic 金 (OC *krɯm).

㾪.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“illness”) + phonetic 省 (OC *sreŋʔ, *sleŋʔ).
Related to 省 (OC *sreŋʔ, “to reduce”) (Wang, 1982).

㾴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“sickness”) + phonetic 查 (OC *ʔsraː, *zraː, *zraː)

㾵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“sickness”) + phonetic 計 (OC *kiːds)

㾶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“sickness”) + phonetic 骨 (OC *kuːd)

㾷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“sickness”) + phonetic 虒 (OC *sle)

㾻.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“illness”) + phonetic 除 (OC *l'a, *l'as).

㿩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 白 (OC *braːɡ) + semantic 黑 (“light”) – light

㿶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 旁 (OC *baːŋ) + semantic 皮.

䀀.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 氾 (OC *pʰoms, *bom) + semantic 皿 (“vessel”).

䀚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 目 (“eyes”) + phonetic 卬 (OC *ŋaːŋ, *ŋaŋʔ, “man standing and man kneeling”).

䀡.e: To watch 見 and observe 占; 占 also provides the pronunciation

䂍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 矛 (“spear; lance”) + phonetic 暴 (OC *boːwɢs, *boːɡ).

䂖.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䂖 – see 石 (“stone; rock; gem; jewel; jade; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 石).

䂫.e: A load that takes great 大 strength 力

䂷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 石 + phonetic 乖 (OC *kʷrɯːl).

䃎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 石 (“stone; rock”) + phonetic 奓 (OC *ʔr'aːl, *ʔr'aːls).

䃼.e: Recorded as an unorthodox character in Longkan Shoujian 《龍龕手鑑·礻部·平聲》.

䄍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 示 + phonetic 昔 (OC *sjaːɡ).

䄩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 禾 (“grain”) + phonetic 弋 (OC *lɯɡ).

䄮.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 禾 (“grain”) + phonetic 夫 (OC *pa, *ba).

䄷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 禾 (“grain”) + phonetic 石 (OC *djaɡ).

䆋.e: The time of year when farmers would burn 禾 crops 火

䆫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 穴 + phonetic 怱 (OC *sʰloːŋ).

䇂.e: Pictograph of a curved knife-like object.

䇓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 須 (OC *so) + semantic 立.

䇫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 竹 (“bamboo”) + phonetic 𦣞 (yí).

䈰.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䈰 – see 筲.
(This character is a variant form of 筲).

䉷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 竹 (“bamboo”) + phonetic 嚴 (OC *ŋam).

䊆.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 臼 (OC *ɡuʔ, “mortar”) + semantic 米 (“rice”).

䊜.e: A rice 米 ball 團; 團 also provides the pronunciation

䋎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 糸 + phonetic 旦 (OC *taːns).

䋞.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 糸 (“silk”) + phonetic 网 (OC *mlaŋʔ, “net”).

䋹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 糸 + phonetic 畐 (OC *pʰrɯɡ, *bɯɡ).

䋽.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 糸 + phonetic 封 (OC *poŋ, *poŋs).

䌈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 糸 + phonetic 𦐇 (tà).

䌛.e: Possibly phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声), with an ancient form of 鼬 (OC *lɯwɢs) acting as the phonetic component.

䌸.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *donʔ, *dons): semantic 糸 (“rope”) + phonetic 專 (OC *tjon).

䍇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 缶 (“crock; pottery”) + phonetic 乏.

䍤.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 罒 + phonetic 齊 (OC *zliːl, *zliːls).

䎑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 羽 (“wings”) + phonetic 彔 (OC *b·roːɡ).

䎚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 羽 (“wings”) + phonetic 慧 (OC *ɡʷeːds).

䏆.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 耳 (“ear”) + phonetic 曹 (OC *zluː).

䏝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tjon, *tjonʔ, *djonʔ): semantic 肉 (“body”) + phonetic 專 (OC *tjon).

䏻.e: Calligraphic variant. Simplified from 能 (𫧇 → 去).

䐥.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic ⺼ (“meat”) + phonetic 翁 (OC *qloːŋ).

䑦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 舟 (“boat”) + phonetic 句 (OC *koː, *koːs, *kos, *ɡo).

䒆.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 舟 (“boat”) + phonetic 徹 (OC *tʰed).

䒢.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 艸 (“grass”) + phonetic 卬 (OC *ŋaːŋ, *ŋaŋʔ).

䒩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 艸 (“grass; plant”) + phonetic 禾 (OC *ɡoːl).

䒿.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *reːw): phonetic 勞 (OC *raːw, *raːws) + semantic 肉 (“meat”).

䓁.e: Calligraphic variant. Simplified from 等 (𥫗 → 艹).

䓕.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䓕 – see 薳.
(This character is the simplified form of 薳).
 Notes:
Simplified Chinese is mainly used in Mainland China, Malaysia, and Singapore.
Traditional Chinese is mainly used in Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan.

䓠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 艹 + phonetic 沓 (OC *duːb).

䓪.e: Coined by parents of Rosanna Wong

䓴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 艸 (“grass; plant”) + phonetic 耎 (OC *njonʔ).

䔺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 艸 (“grass; plant”) + phonetic 隋 (OC *l̥ʰoːlʔ, *l'oːlʔ, *ljol).

䖈.e: A tiger 虍 baring its claws 彐

䖍.e:
According to the Shuowen Jiezi, Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡren): phonetic 文 (OC *mɯn) + semantic 虍.
According to Li Xueqin (2012), the phonetic component is instead 虍 (OC *qʰaː).

䖒.e: Phonosemantic compound. 虍 (hū; tiger) represents the sound and 豆 (bean) represents the meaning.

䖙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 臥 (“lie down”) + phonetic 虒

䖦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 虫 (“insect; worm”) + phonetic 出 (OC *kʰljuds, *kʰljud).

䗚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 虫 (“insect”) + phonetic 尃 (OC *pʰa).

䗛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 虫 (“insect”) + phonetic 脩 (OC *slɯw).

䗜.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 虫 (“insect”) + phonetic 留 (OC *m·ru, *m·rus).

䗟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 殹 (OC *qiːs) + semantic 虫 (“insect”).

䗡.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 虫 (“insect”) + phonetic 焉 (OC *qran, *ɢan, *qan).

䗽.e:
Austroasiatic (Norman & Mei, 1976; Schuessler, 2007). Compare Proto-Austroasiatic *roaj (“"fly (insect)"”) and its descendants.
Foreign (pre-)initial *r- was lost before Old Chinese *w-.

䘆.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 虫 (“insect; worm”) + phonetic 憲 (OC *hŋans)

䘑.e: Long 永 vessels through one's body ⺼

䘙.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 行 + phonetic 韋 (OC *ɢʷɯl) + semantic 帀.

䘥.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 衤 + phonetic 甲 (OC *kraːb).

䘼.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 衣 (“clothing”) + phonetic 宛 (OC *qon, *qonʔ).
Perhaps related to 腕 (OC *qoːns, “wrist”) (Schuessler, 2007).

䙍.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 衣 (“clothing”) + phonetic 飛 (bi).
The "clothing" radical was added to distinguish it from 飛 /飞 (fēi), perhaps taking the radical of the subsequent 褙 (bèi).

䙎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 衤 + phonetic 奚 (OC *ɡeː).

䙳.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䙳 – see 熛 (“flame flying in all directions; rapid; swift; violent”).
(This character is a variant form of 熛).

䛡.e: A spoken 言 tongue 舌; 舌 also provides the pronunciation

䛻.e:
Possibly phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声) originally; see below.
Possibly a cognate to 誘 (OC *luʔ) originally, but later normalized as a variant character thereof, losing its own phonetic value (Wang, 2012).

䛼.e: Slanderous 毁 words 言; 毁 also provides the pronunciation

䜈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 言 (“speech”) + phonetic 巢 (OC *zraːw, *zraːws).

䜌.e: A spool 言 bound by thread 糹

䜑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声) and ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): semantic 言 + phonetic 惡 (OC *qaː, *qaːɡs, *qaːɡ).

䜙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 訁 (“speech”) + phonetic 㬎 (xiǎn).

䜻.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 豆 (“bean”) + phonetic 是 (OC *djeʔ).

䝋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsloːŋ, *ʔsloŋ): semantic 豕 + phonetic 從 (OC *zloŋ, *zloŋ, *zloŋs)

䝤.e: Hunting with dogs 犭 and torches 尞; 尞 also provides the pronunciation

䞐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hljunʔ): semantic 貝 (“shell; money”) + phonetic 春 (OC *tʰjun).

䞰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 走 (“run”) + phonetic 赤 (OC *kʰljaɡ).

䟃.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 走 (“to run”) + phonetic 參 (OC *sʰluːm, *sʰluːms, *sloːm, *sʰrum, *srum).

䟄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 走 (“run”) + phonetic 責 (OC *ʔsreːɡ).

䟉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 走 + phonetic 蜀 (OC *djoɡ).

䟒.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 走 + phonetic 雚 (OC *koːns).

䟴.e: Believed to be a nasal-stop alternation of 趷 (ngat6, gat6). (Ts'ou, 1979)

䠊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 𧾷 + phonetic 非 (OC *pɯl).

䠹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 身 + phonetic 亥 (OC *ɡɯːʔ).

䡰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 殸 (OC *kʰreːŋ, *kʰeːŋs) + semantic 車.

䢂.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 車 (“vehicle”) + phonetic 立.
Borrowed from English lift.

䣘.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 堂 (OC *daːŋ) + semantic 阝 (“city”).

䣧.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 酉 + phonetic 弋 (OC *lɯɡ).

䣩.e: Wine 酉 fit to be savored 享

䤝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 金 + phonetic 卬 (OC *ŋaːŋ, *ŋaŋʔ).

䤠.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 鍺 – see 堵 (“ancient unit of measure for sixteen bells or chimes hanging on a bell pendant stand”).
(This character is a variant form of 堵).

䤪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 金 + phonetic 朵 (OC *toːlʔ).

䥏.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 金 (“metal”) + phonetic 御 (OC *ŋas).

䥑.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䥑 – see 錽 (“a kind of decoration on a horse's head”).
(This character is a variant form of 錽).
Borrowed from English meitnerium, via Mandarin.

䥝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 金 (“metal”) + phonetic 麀 (OC *qɯw)

䥥.e: Found in the historical Zhengzitong 《正字通 (Zhèngzìtōng)》 dictionary published during the Qing dynasty (1671 AD).

䦓.e: To watch 見 and observe 占; 占 also provides the pronunciation

䦟.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䦟 – see 穩 (“stable; firm; steady; steady; reliable; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 穩).

䦷.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䦟 – see 穩 (“stable; firm; steady; steady; reliable; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 穩).

䧕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 阝 + phonetic 或 (OC *ɡʷɯːɡ).

䨤.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 雨 (“rain”) + phonetic 迪 (OC *l'ɯːwɢ).

䨥.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䨥 – see 雙 (“two; double; twin; even; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 雙).

䨨.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 雨 (“rain”) + phonetic 追 (OC *tul).

䨮.e:
Found in small seal script (based on Shuowen Jiezi). Also found in clerical script.
Listed in various historical dictionaries such as Longkan Shoujian 《龍龕手鑑》 (997 AD) and Jiyun 《集韻 (Jíyùn)》 (1037 AD).

䩁.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 非 (OC *pɯl) + semantic 毳 (“fine hair on animals”).

䩕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 革 (“leather”) + phonetic 卬 (OC *ŋaːŋ, *ŋaŋʔ).

䩷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 革 + phonetic 旁 (OC *baːŋ).

䪄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 革 (“leather”) + phonetic 黃 (OC *ɡʷaːŋ).

䪤.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 韭 (“garlic chives”) + phonetic 番 (OC *paːl, *paːls, *pʰaːn, *pʰan, *ban).

䪿.e: A skull from above, with 乂 marking the fontanelle

䫀.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 艮 (OC *kɯːns) + semantic 頁 (“head”).

䫃.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䫃 – see 脣 (“lip; lip-shaped thing; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 脣).

䫉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): abbreviated phonetic 豹 (OC *preːwɢs) + semantic 頁.

䫌.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 卑 + semantic 頁 (“leaf”).

䫫.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䫫 – see 髏 (“skull; skull”).
(This character is a variant form of 髏).

䬒.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䬒 – see 颼 (“sound of wind; to blow”).
(This character is a variant form of 颼).

䬢.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䬢 – see 饕 (“gluttonous; greedy; covetous”).
(This character is a variant form of 饕).

䬣.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䬣 – see 餼 (“foodgrain as a gift; foodgrain; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 餼).

䬥.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䬥 – see 饐 (“to rot due to moisture”).
(This character is an ancient form of 饐).

䭔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 食 (“to eat”) + phonetic 追 (OC *tul).

䭫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 旨 (OC *kjiʔ) + semantic 首 (“head”).

䭬.e: Based on the Shuowen small seal script.

䭳.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 魏 (OC *ŋɡuls) + semantic 香 (“fragrant”).
Clipping of 阿魏 (ēwèi).

䭶.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䭶 – see 騏 (“dark-blue piebald horse; excellent horse; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 騏).

䭸.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 父 (OC *paʔ, *baʔ).

䭹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 卬 (OC *ŋaːŋ, *ŋaŋʔ).

䭺.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䭺 – see 䭹 (“movement of a horse”).
(This character is a variant form of 䭹).

䭼.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䭼 – see 騏 (“dark-blue piebald horse; excellent horse; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 騏).

䮁.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䮁 – see 駢 (“two horses that are side-by-side; side-by-side; parallel; antithetical”).
(This character is a variant form of 駢).

䮿.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䮿 – see 蹇 (“lame; crippled; difficult; hard; unlucky; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 蹇).

䯊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 骨 (“bone”) + phonetic 可 (OC *kʰaːlʔ).

䰂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 髟 + phonetic 采 (OC *sʰɯːʔ).

䰗.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʷɯ, *kʷruʔ): semantic 鬥 (“to fight; to compete”) + phonetic 龜 (OC *kʷɯ, *kʷrɯ).

䰻.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 魚 (OC *ŋa, “fish”) + semantic 攵.

䱷.e: To fish 魚 in a river氵; 魚 also provides the pronunciation

䲟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qiŋs): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 印 (OC *qiŋs).

䲣.e: To fish 魚 in a river氵; 魚 also provides the pronunciation

䳇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 母 (OC *mɯʔ) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

䳔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 𦥑 (jú) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

䳢.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 其 (OC *kɯ, *ɡɯ) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

䳣.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䳣 – see 鳶 (“kite, especially the black kite; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 鳶).

䴈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 奧 (OC *quːɡs) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

䴬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 麥 (“wheat”) + phonetic 弋 (OC *lɯɡ).

䵣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 黑 (“black”) + phonetic 旦 (OC *taːns).

䵺.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䵺 – see 圢 (“flat, level, smooth”).
(This character is a variant form of 圢).

䶬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 幵 (OC *kŋeːn) + semantic 龍 (“dragon”).

䶮.e: Liu Yan's original name was 劉巖 /刘岩 (liú yán). He changed 巖 /岩 (yán) to 龑, a character he invented. The character refers to a saying in the I Ching, 飛龍在天 ("the flying dragon in the sky").

䶸.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䶸 – see 吆 (“to yell”).
(This character is a variant form of 吆).

一.e: Represents heaven (天), earth (旦), or the number 1

七.e: Originally resembled a cross, depicting the meaning "to cut", since 七 sounds like 切 (qiē; to cut).

丄.e: One stroke on top of another; variant of 上

万.e: Origin unclear. Simplified form of 萬.

丈.e: Depicts 10 (十) hand-lengths (又): 丈 is a traditional Chinese unit of length equivalent to about 3 meters.

三.e: Three  parallel lines; compare 一 (one) and 二 (two)

上.e: One stroke on top of another; compare 下 (below)

下.e: One stroke under another; compare 上 (above)

不.e: A bird flying toward the sky 一

与.e: Simplified form of 與.  Phonosemantic compound. 舁 represents the meaning and 牙 represents the sound.  The traditional component 舁 was removed for simplification.

丐.e: A person leaning forward to ask for help

丑.e: Pictograph of an animal's paw. In simplified Chinese 丑 is also used to mean "ugly", while in traditional Chinese this meaning is written with a separate character 醜.

专.e: Depicts a hand (寸) spinning a wheel (叀). Based on the original meaning "turn", now written as 轉. The current meaning is a phonetic loan. The simplified character 专 was adapted from the cursive form of the traditional character 專.

丕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pʰrɯ): phonetic 不 (OC *pɯ, *pɯʔ, *pɯ') + semantic 一

丙.e: Origin unclear. Variously explained as a table or pedestal, or a fish tail.

业.e: Simplified form of 業.  Origin unclear.

东.e: Simplified form of 東, the sun 日 rising behind a tree 木

丝.e: Simplified form of 絲, two threads

丟.e: A horizontal line added to the sematically similar character 去 (leave; go).

両.e: Two people 入 together in a cart

丢.e: A lost 厶 jade 玉

两.e: Two people 从 together in a cart

严.e: Simplified form of 嚴.  Phonosemantic compound. 喦 represents the meaning and 敢 represents the sound. Based on the original meaning "boast". The meaning later shifted to "extremely" and "severe". The traditional component 敢 was removed for simplification.

丧.e: Simplified form of 喪; to cry 哭 over the dead 亡

丩.e: Pictograph of two ropes entangled together, now written as 糾.

个.e: A tally 丨 of people 人

丬.e: 丬 is a component form of 爿.

中.e: A line 丨 through the center of a box 口

丮.e: Pictograph of a person with both hands outstretched.

Pictogram of something carved/engraved. Proto-form of 㓞. Unrelated to 丰.
The character was also used as a variant of 王, later written as 玉.

串.e: Two objects 口 strung 丨 together

丳.e: Variant/corruption of 𠁷, which is a duplication of 串.

丽.e: Simplified form of 麗; the antlers of a deer 鹿

乌.e: Simplified form of 烏; a crow; compare 鸟

Orthographic borrowing from Korean 乭 (dol, 돌).
Spelling pronunciation, as 石 (shí).

乯.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Korean 乯.
Spelling pronunciation, as 乎 (hū).

乸.e:
Probably a dialectal form of 女 (OC *naʔ, “woman; female”) (Schuessler, 2007).
Alternatively, Li (1990) proposed that this may be a Tai substrate loan, related to Zhuang nax (“younger maternal uncle or aunt”), Thai น้า (náa) (which go back to Proto-Tai *naːꟲ (“mother's younger sibling”)).

For pronunciation and definitions of 亓 – see 其 (“his; her; its; their; he; she; it; they; one; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 其).
Clipping of 亓官 (Qíguān), which is a corrupted form of 笄官 (“an ancient official position”)

備.e: Pictograph of a container for holding arrows, which helped in preparation for battle. The 亻 (person) component was added later.

傚.e: To effect 效 another person 亻; 效 also provides the pronunciation

傛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *loŋ, *loŋʔ): semantic 人 (“man”) + phonetic 容 (OC *loŋ)

傧.e: Someone 亻 who hosts guests 宾; 宾 also provides the pronunciation

傫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 累 (OC *ruːls, *rolʔ, *rols).

傯.e: Someone 亻 in a rush 悤; 悤 also provides the pronunciation

傱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sloŋʔ): semantic 亻 (“person”) + phonetic 從 (OC *zloŋ, *zloŋ, *zloŋs).

債.e: A person's 亻 duty 責; 責 also provides the pronunciation

傷.e: Phonosemantic compound. 亻 and 刀 represent the meaning and 昜 represents the sound.

傻.e: A man 亻 being hit 夂 on the head 囟

僆.e: Unclear. Compare Lepcha ᰜᰤᰬᰵ (lyeŋ, “young”), Iu Mien jaih jaanx (“a hen which has not laid eggs”), Proto-Tai *C̬.qɤːŋᴮ (“young hen”), Proto-Hlai *C-ləŋ (“chicken classifier”) (whence Hlai lang) (Schuessler, 2007).

僇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rɯwɢs, *rɯwɢ): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 翏 (OC *ɡ·rɯːw, *ɡ·rɯws).

僉.e: Many people 从 talking 口 under one roof 亼

働.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 働.
Spelling pronunciation as 動 /动 (dòng).

僎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sɡronʔ, *sɡrons, *skun): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 巽 (OC *sqʰuːns).

僓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l̥ʰuːlʔ, *ɡluːds): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 貴 (OC *kluds).

僔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsuːnʔ): semantic 人 (“people”) + phonetic 尊 (OC *ʔsuːn)

僖.e: Someone 亻 in love 喜; 喜 also provides the pronunciation

僗.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 僗 – see 勞 (“to toil; to do manual labour; to trouble; to disturb; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 勞).

僝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sɡreːn, *sɡʷrens): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 孱 (OC *sɡreːn, *sɡreːnʔ, *sɡren).

僞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋʷrals): semantic 亻 (“person”) + phonetic 爲 (OC *ɢʷal, *ɢʷal, *ɢʷals).

僤.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *daːnʔ, *daːns, *djan): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 單 (OC *taːn, *djan, *djanʔ, *djans).

僫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声) and ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): semantic 亻 + phonetic 惡 (OC *qaː, *qaːɡs, *qaːɡ).

僮.e: A servant boy 童; 童 also provides the pronunciation

僰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *bɯːɡ, *brɯɡɡ): phonetic 棘 (OC *krɯɡ) + semantic 人 (“person; people”).

僱.e: To hire 雇 someone 亻; 雇 also provides the pronunciation

僸.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *krɯms, *ŋɡrɯmʔ): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 禁 (OC *krɯm, *krɯms).

價.e: A merchant 亻賈; 賈 also provides the pronunciation

僻.e: people living out of the way

儃.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *daːn, *djan): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 亶 (OC *taːnʔ).

儈.e: A person 亻 who organizes a meeting 會

儌.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kleːwʔ, *kleːwʔ): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 敫 (OC *kleːwɢs, *ɢlewɢ, *kleːwɢ).

儍.e: A man 亻 being hit 夂 on the head 囟

儑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋɯːms, *ŋɯːb, *ŋaːb): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 㬎 (xiǎn).

儓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tʰɯːs, *dɯː): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 臺 (OC *dɯː).

儥.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'oːɡ): semantic 亻 (“person”) + phonetic 𧶠 (OC *luɡ).

儰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷalʔ): semantic 人 (“person”) + phonetic 蔿 (OC *ɢʷalʔ)

儵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hljɯwɢ): phonetic 攸 (OC *lɯw) + semantic 黑 (“black”).

儽.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ruːls, *rul): semantic 亻 (“human”) + phonetic 纍 (OC *rul, *ruls).

儿.e: Simplified form of 兒, a picture of a child; compare 人

兀.e: Cutting 一 off the feet 儿

允.e: Pictograph of a person nodding their head in agreement.

元.e: A man 儿 with two lines emphasizing their head 二

兄.e: Older brother 儿 speaking 口 to younger ones

充.e: Origin unclear. The 子 (child) component is upside-down.

兆.e: Cracks in oracle bones used in fortune-telling

兇.e: A container 凵 of wickedness 乂

先.e: Someone stepping 止 in front of another person 儿

光.e: A person 儿 carrying a torch 火

克.e: A weapon 十 used to subdue a beast 兄

兌.e: A beast 兄 with two horns 丷

免.e: Pictograph of a person with a hat, now written as 冕. The current meaning is a phonetic loan.

兑.e: A beast 兄 with two horns 丷

兓.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): duplication of 兂 ("hairpin") – sharp.

兛.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 克 (“gram”) + 千 (“one thousand”)

兜.e: A man wearing a helmet, now meaning "pouch"

兟.e: Two people 先 marching; 先 also provides the pronunciation

兢.e: Possibly the vocalic variant of 驚 (OC *kreŋ, “to be afraid; to alarm”) (Schuessler, 2007). Cognate with 謹 (OC *kɯnʔ, “to be careful; to be attentive”).

兦.e: A man 人 in a coffin; see 亾

內.e: A man 入  entering a door 冂

全.e: Jade 玉 put away 入 for safe-keeping

兩.e: Two people 入 together in a cart

公.e: To divide 八 what is private 厶

六.e: Originally a pictograph of a tent or building. Unrelated to modern meaning.

兮.e: Depicts two lines of air (八) coming from breath (丂).

共.e: Two hands 八 holding one object 廾

关.e: Phonosemantic compound. 門 represents the meaning and 𢇇 represents the sound.  The simplified character 关 was adapted from the cursive form of the traditional character 關. The traditional component 門 was removed for simplification.

兴.e: Depicts four hands (舁) working together (同) to raise something. The simplified character 兴 was adapted from the cursive form of the traditional character 興.

兵.e: Two hands 八 holding an axe 丘

其.e: Pictograph of a basket, now written as 箕. The current meaning is a phonetic loan.

具.e: Two hands 八 writing on a tablet 目

典.e: Two hands 八 holding a book 冊

兼.e: A hand holding two sheafs of grain 禾

兽.e: An animal with horns 丷, eyes 田, nose 一, and mouth 口

冁.e: When an individual 单 opens up 展; 展 also provides the pronunciation

冂.e: Pictograph of a large room or platform. Original form of 堂.

冃.e: Represents a hat. Top component form of 冒.

冄.e: 冄 is a component form of 冉.

内.e: A man 人  entering a door 冂

冈.e: Simplified form of 岡, clouds 网 forming over a mountain 山

冉.e: Pictograph of two strands of something drooping.

冊.e: Bamboo strips 冂 tied together to form a book; compare 册

冋.e: A plot of land 口 surrounded by a border 冂; 冂 also provides the pronunciation

册.e: Bamboo strips 冂 tied together to form a book

再.e: Two fish 冉 caught on a line 一

冑.e: A man ⺼ wearing a helmet 由

冒.e: Pictograph of a hat over an eye. Old way of writing 帽 (hat).

冓.e: Pictograph of two fish facing each other.

冗.e: A cover-cloth 冖 on a table 几

写.e: Phonosemantic compound. 宀 represents the meaning and 舄 represents the sound. The contribution of the 宀 (roof) component to the meaning is unclear. Perhaps used since writing is typically done indoors. The simplified character 写 was adapted from the cursive form of the traditional character 寫.

军.e: Soldiers 冖 (distorted 力) in a cart 车

农.e: Simplified form of 農, tilling the field 田 with a hoe 辰

冞.e: Han compound: Kangxi radical 冖 (lid) + phonetic component 米.

冟.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hmjeɡ): phonetic 冖 (OC *meːɡ, “cover”) + semantic 皀 (“container of rice”).

冠.e: A man 元 puts on a cap 冖 with his hand 寸

冡.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 𠔼 + 豕, a pig under either a roof or a hat. Similar to 家 (in which however the male pig acts as a phonetic component).

冤.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 冖 (“cover”) + 兔 (“rabbit”).

冥.e: The sun 日, covered 冖, with 六 providing the pronunciation

冧.e: Probably a Kra-Dai substrate word. Compare Proto-Tai *lɤmꟲ, Thai ล้ม (lóm, “to fall”), Zhuang laemx (“to fall”), Maonan lam⁵ (“to fall”).

冩.e: Phonosemantic compound. 宀 represents the meaning and 舄 represents the sound. The contribution of the 宀 (roof) component to the meaning is unclear. Perhaps used since writing is typically done indoors.

冪.e: A cover 冖 cloth 幕; 冖 also provides the pronunciation

冫.e: 冫 is a component form of 冰.

冬.e: A man walking 夂 on ice ⺀

冱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 氵 + phonetic 互 (OC *ɡaːs).

决.e: Simplified form of 決.  Phonosemantic compound. 氵 represents the meaning and 夬 represents the sound. Based on the original meaning "burst open". The meaning later shifted to "clear", "cut off", "decide" and "judge".

况.e: Simplified form of 況.  Phonosemantic compound. 氵 represents the meaning and 兄 represents the sound.

冶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *laːʔ): semantic 冫 (“ice”) + phonetic 台 (OC *l̥ʰɯː, *l'ɯː, *l'ɯː, *lɯ). It expresses the idea of ice softening and melting, and by extension, of metal melting.

凊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰleŋs): semantic 冫 (“ice”) + phonetic 青 (OC *sʰleːŋ).

凓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *riɡ): semantic 冫 (“ice”) + phonetic 栗 (OC *riɡ).

凔.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰaːŋ): semantic 冫 (“ice”) + phonetic 倉 (OC *sʰaːŋ).
Probably from 涼 (OC *ɡ·raŋ, *ɡ·raŋs, *ɡ·raŋs, “cold”). Also note similarities to Burmese ဆောင်း (hcaung:, “winter”).

凘.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *se): semantic 冫 (“ice”) + phonetic 斯 (OC *se).

凞.e: Found in various historical dictionaries such as 《四聲篇海》 (Jin dynasty), 《篇海類篇》 and 《重訂直音篇》 (Ming dynasty) and 《字彙補》 (Qing dynasty).

凟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 冫 + phonetic 賣 (OC *mreːs).

凡.e: A plate or flat dish; an everyday item

凢.e: A plate or flat dish; an everyday item

凣.e: A plate or flat dish; an everyday item

凪.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 凪 (nagi).
Spelling pronunciation, as 止 (zhǐ).

凭.e: Leaning on (trusting) 任 a table 几

凯.e: Simplified form of 凱.  Phonosemantic compound. 几 represents the meaning and 豈 represents the sound.

凰.e: Phonosemantic compound. 几 represents the meaning and 皇 represents the sound.

凱.e: Phonosemantic compound. 几 represents the meaning and 豈 represents the sound.

凶.e: A container 凵 of wickedness 乂

凷.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 土 (“earth”) + 凵 (“pit”) – a clod of dirt.

出.e: A sprout 屮 growing out of a container 凵

击.e: Simplified form of 擊; 手 provides the meaning

凼.e: Water 水 in a container 凵

函.e: Letters placed in a container 凵

凾.e: Letters placed in a container 凵

凿.e: A drill 丵 making a hole 凵

刂.e: 刂 is a component form of 刀.

刃.e: A knife 刀 sharp enough to draw blood 丶

刄.e: A knife 刀 sharp enough to draw blood 丶

分.e: Pieces 八 being further subdivided with a knife 刀

刋.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 刋 – see 刊 (“to cut off; to peel off; to carve; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 刊).

刑.e: A man 刂 in jail 开

划.e: Phonosemantic compound. 刂 represents the meaning and 戈 represents the sound. In simplified Chinese 划 is also used to mean "cut; slash; draw line", while in traditional Chinese this meaning is written with a separate character 劃.

则.e: Laws inscribed 刂 on a slate 贝

初.e: Cutting 刀 the cloth 衤 is the first step in making clothes

删.e: To cut 刂 out passages from a book 册

刣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 台 + semantic 刂 (“knife”).

別.e: To draw boundaries 刂 between classes 另

刪.e: To cut 刂 out passages from a book 冊

别.e: To draw boundaries 刂 between classes 另

刱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰraŋs): phonetic 井 (OC *skeŋʔ) + phonetic 刅 (OC *sʰraŋ).

刷.e: A person bent over 尸 with a towel 巾

剄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *keːŋʔ): phonetic 巠 (OC *keːŋ) + semantic 刂 (“knife”)

剅.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *toː, *roː): phonetic 豆 (OC *doːs) + semantic 刂 (“knife”).

則.e: Laws inscribed 刂 on a slate 貝

剋.e: A weapon 刂 used to subdue 十 a beast 兄

前.e: Depicts a boat (舟) moving (止) forward (前).

剕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 非 (OC *pɯl) + semantic 刂.

剚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsrɯs): phonetic 事 (OC *ʔsrɯs, *zrɯs) + semantic 刀 (“knife; sword”).

剬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *toːn, *tjonʔ): phonetic 耑 (OC *toːn) + semantic 刂 (“knife”).

剴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kŋɯːl, *ŋɯːl): phonetic 豈 (OC *ŋ̊ʰɯːlʔ, *ŋ̊ʰɯlʔ) + semantic 刂 (“knife”).

剼.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 參 (OC *sʰluːm, *sʰluːms, *sloːm, *sʰrum, *srum) + semantic 刂 (“knife”).

剾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰoː, *qoː): phonetic 區 (OC *qoː, *kʰo) + semantic 刀 (“knife”)

劆.e: Found in the historical Zhengzitong 《正字通 (Zhèngzìtōng)》 dictionary published during the Qing dynasty (1671 AD).

劋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsawʔ, *ʔseːwʔ): phonetic 喿 (OC *saːws) + semantic 刂 (“knife”).

劎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 僉 (OC *skʰlam) + semantic 刄.

劓.e: To cut 刂 the nose 鼻; 鼻 also provides the pronunciation

劔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 僉 (OC *skʰlam) + semantic 刄.

劗.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsoːn, *zoːn): phonetic 贊 (OC *ʔsaːns) + semantic 刀 (“knife”).
Possibly cognate with 剪 (OC *ʔslenʔ, “to cut”) (Wang, 1982).

劙.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *reːlʔ, *reːls, *rel): phonetic 蠡 (OC *reːlʔ, *rel, *roːl) + semantic 刂 (“knife”).
Possibly related to 刕 (lí).

劝.e: The power 力 beside 又 the throne

功.e: Results produced by capable 力 labor 工; 工 also provides the pronunciation

加.e: Supporting a cause with the strength 力 of one's words 口

务.e: Simplified form of 務.  Phonosemantic compount. 矛 represents the sound, while 攵 and 力 both represent the meaning. The traditional component 矛 was removed for simplification.

劥.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰraːŋ): phonetic 亢 (OC *kaːŋ, *kʰaːŋs) + semantic 力 (“strength”).

劳.e: A man lifting 力 a load 冖 of grass 艹

効.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡreːws): phonetic 交 (OC *kreːw) + semantic 力

勞.e: A man lifting 力 a load 冖 of grass 炏

勠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rɯw, *rɯwɢs, *rɯwɢ): phonetic 翏 (OC *ɡ·rɯːw, *ɡ·rɯws) + semantic 力 (“strength”).

勥.e: Perhaps from Nanking court dialect Mandarin 強 /强 according to Ogawa (1932).

勶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 徹 (OC *tʰed) + semantic 力 (“force”).

勷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *njaŋ): phonetic 襄 (OC *snaŋ) + semantic 力 (“power”).

勹.e: 勹 is a component form of 包.

勺.e: A spoon 勹 with something in it 丶

勻.e: Using a spoon 勹 to measure out two equal parts 二

勾.e: 勾 is a component form of 句.

勿.e: A knife 勹 cutting fragments 丿 off

匀.e: Using a spoon 勹 to measure out two equal parts 冫

匁.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 匁(もんめ) (monme)
Pronunciation borrowed from 兩／两 (liǎng).

匃.e: Variant form of 丐. Origin unclear. Possibly depicts a beggar holding his hand out.

包.e: To swaddle 勹 a baby 巳; 勹 also provides the pronunciation

匉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pʰreːŋ): semantic 勹 + phonetic 平 (OC *ben, *beŋ).

匊.e: A handful of rice 米 in a bowl 勹

化.e: Pictograph of a right-side up person and an upside-down person, depicting the meaning "turn around" or "transform".

北.e: Two people 匕 sitting back-to-back; phonetic loan for "north"

匜.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *lal, *lalʔ) and ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): semantic 匚 (“container”) + phonetic 也 (OC *laːlʔ).

匝.e: A geometrical variant of 迊 from its cursive written form.

匳.e: A man 大 packing a suitcase 區

匵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'oːɡ): semantic 匚 + phonetic 𧶠 (OC *luɡ)

区.e: A place 匸 under a lord's control 乂

匾.e: A board 扁 made of bamboo 匸; 扁 also provides the pronunciation

區.e: A place 匸 where many mouths 品 live

十.e: A single vertical line, representing one set of ten. Analagous to the archaic characters 廿 (twenty) and 卅 (thirty).

千.e: Phonosemantic compound. 一 represents the meaning and 亻 represents the sound.

卄.e: Two tens 十 side by side

卅.e: Three tally marks; compare ten 十 and twenty 廿

升.e: Pictograph of a spoon, a utensil used to pick things up.

午.e: Pictograph of a pestle used for pounding rice, now written as 杵. The current meaning "noon" is a phonetic loan.

卉.e: Ten 十 different types of grass 艹

半.e: A cow 牛 splitting 丷 wood in half

卌.e: Four tally marks; compare ten 十, twenty 廿, and thirty 卅

卍.e: Origin unclear. Simplified form of 萬.

卐.e: Origin unclear. Simplified form of 萬.

卑.e: A hand 又(altered) holding an empty shell 甲 (altered)

卓.e: To foresee ⺊ the dawn 早 coming; 早 also provides the pronunciation

单.e: Simplified form of 單.  Pictograph of a hunting or military trap constructed from two rocks and a net. The current meaning "single" is a phonetic loan.

卜.e: A crack on an oracle bone

卟.e: To speak 口 of the future 卜; 卜 also provides the pronunciation

卡.e: A card squeezed between 上 and 下

卢.e: Simplified form of 盧.  Phonosemantic compound. 虍 represents the sound, and the remainder of the character is a pictograph of a vessel for cooking rice. The traditional component 皿 was removed for simplification.

卤.e: A container used to brine pickles

卧.e: A person 卜 (altered form of 人) laying in bed 臣

卩.e: A rolled-up scroll or kneeling person

卫.e: Simplified from the cursive form of the traditional character 衛. Depicts feet (止) walking around, patrolling a street intersection (行).

卬.e: A person kneeling 卩 before a person (a king) sitting 匕

卭.e: A person kneeling 卩 before a person (a king) sitting 匕

卯.e: Origin unclear. Variously explained as a mortise (a slot cut into a piece of wood), and knives used for sacrificing animals.

印.e: A hand 爪 stamping a seal 卩

危.e: A person 㔾 at the edge of a cliff 厃; 厃 also provides the pronunciation

即.e: A hungry person卩 kneeling over food 皀; compare 既

卽.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 皀 + phonetic 卪 (OC *ʔsiːɡ).

厂.e: Pictograph of a cliff or similarly shaped object. In simplified Chinese 厂 is also used to mean "factory", while in traditional Chinese this meaning is written with a separate character 廠. In simplified characters the 广 component is often written as 厂.

厃.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 人 (“person”) + 厂 (“cliff”).

厄.e: A person 㔾 being crushed under pressure 厂

厉.e: Simplified form of 厲.  Phonosemantic compound. 厂 represents the meaning and 萬 represents the sound.

厊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋraːʔ): semantic 厂 + phonetic 牙 (OC *ŋraː).

压.e: Soil 土 crushed under pressure 厂

厓.e: A cliff 厂 formed of earth 土

厖.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mroːŋ): semantic 厂 (“cliff”) + phonetic 尨 (OC *mroːŋ).

厗.e: A bitter 辛 mineral 厂

厚.e: Sunlight 日 and children 子 in a building 厂

厝.e: A building 厂 containing the past 昔

厤.e: Original form of 歷. Origin unclear.

厥.e: Bodman (1980) relates it to Tibetan ཁྱོད (khyod, “you”).

厰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 敞 (OC *tʰjaŋʔ) + semantic 厂.

厲.e: Phonosemantic compound. 厂 represents the meaning and 萬 represents the sound.

厴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qemʔ): phonetic 厭 (OC *qemʔ, *qems, *qeb) + semantic 甲 (“shell”).

厷.e: A bent arm; flexing the muscles

去.e: Origin unclear. The 口 component is variously explained as (1) an opening to a building or cave that the person is departing from, or (2) an anus, i.e. the orifice through which things leave a person.

县.e: Simplified form of 縣.  Pictograph of a severed head (首) hanging on a rope (系) from a tree (木). Based on the original meaning "hang" or "suspend". The current meaning is a phonetic loan. The traditional component 系 was removed for simplification.

叁.e: Three 三 with accents to prevent forgery

参.e: Simplified form of 參.  Depicts three stars joined together; in Chinese astronomy 參 is a constellation of three stars. 彡 is a sound component.

參.e: Depicts three stars joined together; in Chinese astronomy 參 is a constellation of three stars. 彡 is a sound component.

叅.e: Depicts three stars joined together; in Chinese astronomy 參 is a constellation of three stars. 彡 is a sound component.

叆.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qɯːds, *qɯlʔ): semantic 雲 (“cloud”) + phonetic 愛 (OC *qɯːds)

又.e: A right hand, representing a pair; compare 左

叉.e: A hand 又 grasping an object 丶

及.e: Pictograph of a hand (又) reaching to grab a person (人).

友.e: Two hands 又 joined, representing friendship; 又 also provides the pronunciation

反.e: A hand 又 held up against a cliff 厂

发.e: Simplified form of both 發 (send out; to issue) and 髮 (hair). Derived from 犬 (dog), the bottom component of 髮.

叒.e: Three hands 又 joined, representing unity

叕.e: Many hooks 又 forming a net

取.e: A hand 又 taking the ear 耳 of a fallen enemy

受.e: Something 冖 passed from one hand 爫 to another 又

变.e: Simplified form of 變.  Phonosemantic compound. 夂 represents the meaning and 䜌 represents the sound.

叚.e: Original form of 假. Depicts one hand (又) borrowing something from another hand (手).

叟.e: A hand 又 holding up a candle, representing failing vision

叠.e: Clothes 叒 stacked on top of a drawer 冝

叡.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): abbreviated 㕡 (“gully”) + 目 (“eye”) – deep insight.

古.e: Words passing through ten 十 mouths 口

句.e: A phrase 勹 from the mouth 口

另.e: Origin unclear. Possibly derived from the left side of 別, which has a related meaning.

只.e: Simple words 八 coming from the mouth 口

台.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 台 – see 鮐 (“mackerel; old person; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 鮐).

史.e: A hand 乂 using a pen to record a history

右.e: Pictograph of a right hand. The 口 (mouth) component was added later to distinguish the character from 又.

叶.e: Phonosemantic compound. 口 represents the meaning and 十 represents the sound. In simplified Chinese 叶 is also used to mean "leaf; page", while in traditional Chinese this meaning is written with a separate character 葉.

司.e: A person speaking orders 口 and raising their arm

叹.e: A mouth 口 exhaling 又

各.e: Walking 夂 and talking 口

吅.e: Duplicate of 口 (mouth). Depicts multiple mouths making lots of noise.

合.e: Pictograph of two mouths facing each other.

吉.e: The words 口 of a scholar 士

吊.e: A popular variant of 弔; an intermediate obsolete variant is 𨒝.

吋.e: A foreign 口 inch 寸; 寸 also provides the pronunciation

同.e: Sharing a common 凡 tongue 口

名.e: A name called out 口 to hail someone at night 夕

后.e: 后 (empress) and its mirror image 司 (take charge) were originally used interchangeably. The origin of both characters is unclear. In simplified Chinese 后 is also used to mean "after, behind", while in traditional Chinese this meaning is written with a separate character 後.

向.e: Depicts a mouth (口) in a closed space (宀) making an echo sound. Based on the original meaning "echo; sound", now written as 響. The current meaning is a phonetic loan.

君.e: A leader 尹 giving orders 口

吝.e: Compare Tibetan སྲི (sri, “to be thrifty”), Proto-Central-Chin *ren (“to economize; frugal”) (whence Mizo ren) (Geilich, 1994).

吠.e: The sound a dog 犬 makes with their mouth 口

吪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋʷaːl): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 化 (OC *hŋʷraːls).

听.e: Words 口 reaching an ear 斤

启.e: A door 户 being opened 口

吲.e: To stretch 引 the mouth 口; 引 also provides the pronunciation

吳.e: Pictograph of a person (大) standing up, yelling with an open mouth (口).

吴.e: Simplified form of 吳.  Pictograph of a person (大) standing up, yelling with an open mouth (口).

吽.e: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit हूँ (hūm̐), a phonetic interjection used in certain Buddhist terms and mantras.

吿.e: To speak 口 with the force of an ox 牛

呆.e: One with wooden 木 speech 口

呈.e: To speak 口 before a king 王

呉.e: Pictograph of a person (大) standing up, yelling with an open mouth (口).

告.e: To speak 口 with the force of an ox 牛

呙.e: Words 内 coming from a mouth 口

呧.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 氐

周.e: Border 冂 around land 土 with a well 口

呪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tjus): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 兄 (OC *tjuɡs, *tjuɡ). It means “to curse,” and by extension, conveys the idea of a spell or charm.

呫.e: To divine 占 with one's tongue 口

呬.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hrids): semantic 口 + phonetic 四 (OC *hljids).

呴.e: Probably related to 呼 (OC *qʰaː, “to exhale, shout”); see there for more (STEDT).

命.e: An order 令 given by mouth 口

呾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *taːd, *ʔraːd): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 旦 (OC *taːns).

咁.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 甘 (OC *kaːm).
Uncertain. Some postulate that it is the fusion of 個物 /个物 (go³ mat⁶) (Cheung, 2013).

咅.e: Depicts spit coming out of a mouth (口).

咊.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 咊 – see 和 (“peaceful; harmonious; gentle; kind; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 和).

咍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hlɯː): semantic 口 (“mouth; speech”) + phonetic 台 (OC *l̥ʰɯː, *l'ɯː, *l'ɯː, *lɯ)

咒.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tjus): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 兄 (OC *tjuɡs, *tjuɡ). It means “to curse,” and by extension, conveys the idea of a spell or charm.

咠.e: To whisper 口 in someone's ear 耳

咢.e:
The same character as 噩, both ultimately derived from or identical to 喪, see there for more.
Later ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋaːɡ): semantic 吅 (“to speak loudly”) + phonetic 屰 (OC *ŋraɡ, “opposite, against”).

咩.e: The sound a sheep 羊 makes with its mouth 口

咲.e: A person 夭 with a big grin ⺮

咸.e: One 一 voice 口 surrounded by conflict 戈

咹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qaːd): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 安 (OC *qaːn)

咼.e: A jaw 口 bone 冎

品.e: Something that everyone is talking 口 about

哘.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 哘.
Spelling pronunciation, as 行 (xíng).

哥.e: One person talking 可 over another 可

哭.e: To shed a tear from your eyes 口 over a person 犬

哯.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡeːnʔ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 見 (OC *keːns, *ɡeːns).

哶.e: The sound a sheep 羊 makes with its mouth 口

哾.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 哾 – see 歠 (“to drink; to sip; to suck”).
(This character is a variant form of 歠).

唂.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 唂 – see 谷 (“(Cantonese) to pressure”).
(This character is a variant form of 谷).

唅.e: To hold 含 in the mouth 口; 含 also provides the pronunciation

唍.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 唍 – see 莞 (“smiling”).
(This character is an ancient form of 莞).

唐.e: Children learning how to read 口 and write 肀 in school 广

唜.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Korean 唜.
Spelling pronunciation, as 末 (mò).

唢.e: A mouth 口 blowing ⺌ through a conch 贝

唦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 沙 (OC *sraːl, *sraːls)

售.e: A street vendor talks 口 incessantly, like a bird 隹

唯.e: Phonosemantic compound. 口 represents the meaning and 隹 represents the sound.

唻.e: Unglottalized form of 了, or 哉 which underwent lenition.

啓.e: To knock 攵 at the door 户

啟.e: To knock 攵 on a door 启; 启 also provides the pronunciation

啬.e: Simplified form of 嗇.  Pictograph of wheat (麥) being stored in a granary. Based on the original meaning "harvest; hoard". The meaning later shifted to "stingy".

啲.e: Possibly derived from 滴 (“drop; a very small quantity”). Compare the semantics of 點／点 (diǎn): "drop (of liquid)" > "a little bit". Also compare Zhuang di.

啵.e: The sound 口 of ripples in the water 波; 波 also provides the pronunciation

善.e: To give someone food 羊 and conversation 口

喆.e: Extremely lucky 吉; 吉 also provides the pronunciation

喌.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tju, *tjuɡ): semantic 吅 + phonetic 州 (OC *tju).

喎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰʷroːl): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 咼 (OC *kʰʷroːl).

喙.e: A hedgehog's 彖 mouth 口

喜.e: Singing 口 and beating drums 壴

喦.e: Pictograph of three mouths (口) linked together.

喪.e: To cry 哭 over the dead 亡

喫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋ̊ʰeːɡ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 契 (OC *kʰeːds, *kʰeːd, *kʰeːd, *kʰɯd).

單.e: Pictograph of a hunting or military trap constructed from two rocks and a net. The current meaning "single" is a phonetic loan.

喱.e: A thousandth 厘 of a meal 口; 厘 also provides the pronunciation

喺.e: Possibly from the copula 係／系 (hai6) (Chappell and Lü, 2022).

喾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰuːɡ): phonetic 學 (OC *ɡruːɡ) + semantic 告 (“to tell”). 告 (OC *kuːɡs, *kuːɡ) may also be a phonetic component.

喿.e: Several birds 品 chirping in a tree 木

嗅.e: To taste 口 an odor 臭; 臭 also provides the pronunciation

嗇.e: Pictograph of wheat (麥) being stored in a granary. Based on the original meaning "harvest; hoard". The meaning later shifted to "stingy".

嗗.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qruːd): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 骨 (OC *kuːd).

嗤.e: To laugh 蚩 out loud 口; 蚩 also provides the pronunciation

嗩.e: A mouth 口 blowing ⺌ through a conch 貝

嗮.e: Uncertain. Chan (1995) suggests that it is from 澌 (MC sjeH, “to dry up; to finish”).

嗺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔslo, *ʔsluːl, *sluːl, *ʔslul, *ʔslulʔ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 崔 (OC *zluːl, *sʰluːl).

嘂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kiːws): semantic 㗊 + phonetic 丩 (OC *kɯw, *krɯw).

嘐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡ·ruːw, *krɯːw, *qʰrɯːw): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 翏 (OC *ɡ·rɯːw, *ɡ·rɯws).

嘑.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 嘑 – see 滹.
(This character is a variant form of 滹).

嘕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʰran): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 焉 (OC *qran, *ɢan, *qan).

嘥.e: Probably a Kra-Dai substrate word. Compare Zhuang sai (“to waste”).

嘪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mreːʔ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 買 (OC *mreːʔ)

嘵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hŋeːw): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 堯 (OC *ŋeːw).

嘻.e: A happy 喜 sound 口; 喜 also provides the pronunciation

噂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsuːnʔ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 尊 (OC *ʔsuːn).

噇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rdoːŋ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 童 (OC *doːŋ).

噈.e: Characters in the same phonetic series (就) (Zhengzhang, 2003)

噏.e: Possibly related to 吟 (ngam4) via Cantonese nasal-stop alternation.

噤.e: Forbidden 禁 to speak 口; 禁 also provides the pronunciation

器.e: Four cooking vessels 口 guarded by a dog 犬

噩.e: A jade statue 王 with four holes made in it 口

噪.e: The sound 口 of birds chirping 喿; 喿 also provides the pronunciation

噮.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʷeːns): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 睘 (OC *ɡʷeŋ)

嚃.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l̥ʰuːb): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 遝 (OC *l'uːb).

嚗.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *proːwɢ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 暴 (OC *boːwɢs, *boːɡ). However, the phonetic component means "violence; sudden", hence it could add part of the meaning.

嚛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʰloːwɢ, *qʰloːwɢ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 樂 (OC *ŋraːwɢs, *raːwɢ, *ŋraːwɢ).

嚟.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 黎 (lai4)
Vernacular reading of 來 /来.

嚡.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth; colloquial speech”) + phonetic 鞋.

嚣.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 㗊 (“four mouths; to clamor”) + 頁 (“head”) – a clamoring crowd.

嚬.e:
Characters in the same phonetic series (頻) (Zhengzhang, 2003)
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *bin): semantic 口 + phonetic 頻 (OC *bin).

嚮.e: Towards 向 the country 鄉; 鄉 also provides the pronunciation

嚳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰuːɡ): phonetic 學 (OC *ɡruːɡ) + semantic 告 (“to tell”). 告 (OC *kuːɡs, *kuːɡ) may also be a phonetic component.

嚴.e: Phonosemantic compound. 喦 represents the meaning and 敢 represents the sound. Based on the original meaning "boast". The meaning later shifted to "extremely" and "severe".

嚶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qreːŋ): semantic 口 (“mouth”) + phonetic 嬰 (OC *qeŋ).

嚽.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 嚽 – see 啜 (“to taste; to eat; to drink; to sip; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 啜).

嚿.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 口 (“mouth; vernacular”) + phonetic 舊.
Bai (1980) proposes that this word comes from 䊆 (MC gjuwX, “cooked dry rice flour”).

囂.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 㗊 (“four mouths; to clamor”) + 頁 (“head”) – a clamoring crowd.

囍.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 喜 + 喜 (“happiness”). Duplication of 喜, the two characters representing the bride and groom.

囗.e: Pictograph of a wall surrounding an area. Component form of 围 (traditional 圍).

囙.e: Found in the historical Guangyun 《廣韻 (Guǎngyùn)》 rime dictionary compiled during the Song dynasty (1007 AD).

囚.e: A person 人 in a cell 囗

四.e: A child 儿 in a room with four walls 囗

囝.e: A baby 子 in a crib 囗

囟.e: A skull from above, with 乂 marking the fontanelle

因.e: Pictograph of clothing surrounding (囗) a person (人), now written as 裀. The current meaning is a phonetic loan.

囡.e: A girl 女 in a crib 囗

团.e: A lot of talent 才 gathered in one place 囗

囥.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 囗 (“enclosure”) + phonetic 亢 (OC *kaːŋ, *kʰaːŋs).
Compare Proto-Tai *k.raŋᴬ, whence Thai ขัง (kǎng, “to confine; to imprison”) (Manomaivibool, 1976).

囧.e: Originally a pictograph of a window. Nowadays sometimes used as a sad-face emoticon.

囪.e: A fire burning and giving off smoke

囮.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *lu, *ŋʷaːl): semantic 囗 (“to surround; to encircle”) + phonetic 化 (OC *hŋʷraːls)

囯.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 囗 (“to surround”) + 王 (“king”). Earliest attested form in regular script found in 《宋敬業造象記》 (507 AD).

困.e: A fence 囗 built around a tree 木

囱.e: A fire 夂 burning and giving off smoke

囷.e: A bin 囗 for storing grain 禾

囻.e: Coined during the Republican period upon 囯, replacing 王 (wáng, “king”) with 民 (mín, “people”).

国.e: Treasure 玉 within a country's borders 囗

图.e: Simplified form of 圖; a map of a district's 啚 borders 囗

圀.e: One of the Chinese characters created by Empress Wu to demonstrate her power. She was said to have disliked how the traditional form contained 或, which she interpreted as meaning "mislead" (see 惑). In this form, this character consists of 四 (four, also acting as the 囗 enclosure) and 方 (direction).

圂.e: A pig 豕 in a pen 囗

國.e: Treasure 或 within a country's borders 囗

圏.e: Unorthodox variant of 圈 with bottom component of 卷 written 己 (jǐ) instead of 㔾 (xiān).

圔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qroːb): semantic 囗 (“closure”) + phonetic 盍 (OC *ɡaːb).

圖.e: A map of a district's 啚 borders 囗

團.e: A lot of talent 專 gathered in one place 囗

圛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *laːɡ): semantic 囗 + phonetic 睪 (OC *neb, *laːɡ, *kuː)

圜.e: A round 睘 fence 囗

圝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *b·roːn): semantic 囗 + phonetic 䜌 (OC *b·roːn, *b·ron, *b·rons).

圞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *b·roːn): semantic 囗 + phonetic 欒 (OC *b·roːn).

土.e: A lump of clay on a potter's wheel

圠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qriːd): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 乙 (OC *qriɡ).

圢.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tʰeːnʔ, *tʰeːŋʔ): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 丁 (OC *rteːŋ, *teːŋ).

圣.e: Simplified form of 聖; a king 王 of listening 耳  and speaking口

圥.e: Depicts some sort of plant or mushroom mentioned in ancient literature.

圦.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 圦.
As 塊／块 (kuài).

圫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 乇 (OC *ʔr'aːɡ).

圱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 千 (OC *sn̥ʰiːn) + semantic 土.

圲.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 + phonetic 千 (OC *sn̥ʰiːn).

圳.e: A furrow cut in the earth 土 by a stream 川; 川 also provides the pronunciation

圴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 勺 (OC *pljewɢ, *bljewɢ).

圵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 上 (OC *djaŋʔ, *djaŋs).

圶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 大 (OC *daːds, *daːds) + semantic 土 (“earth”).

圷.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 圷 (akutsu).
Spelling pronunciation, as 下 (xià).

圸.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 圸.
Spelling pronunciation, as 山 (shān).

圿.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kreːd): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 介 (OC *kreːds).

坁.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kjeʔ): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 氏 (OC *skeŋ, *kje, *ɡjeʔ).

坄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *doː, *leɡ): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 殳 (OC *djo).

坅.e: Possibly from Proto-Sino-Tibetan *qim (“house; pit; womb”); see there for more cognates.

坈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 冗 (OC *njoŋʔ).

坉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *duːn): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 屯 (OC *duːn, *tun).

坸.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 坸 – see 垢 (“filth; dirt; filthy; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 垢).

垆.e: A cottage 卢 with earthen walls 土; 卢 also provides the pronunciation

垈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 代 (OC *l'ɯːɡs) + semantic 土 (“earth; dust”).

垍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡrids): semantic 土 (“earth; soil”) + phonetic 自 (OC *ɦljids).

垚.e: A mound 土 of clay 土

垱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 當 (OC *taːŋ, *taːŋs).

垳.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 垳 (gake).
Spelling pronunciation, as 行 (xíng).

垵.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 垵 – see 埯 (“a hole to dibble seeds; to dibble into holes; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 埯).

垺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 孚 (OC *pʰuw).

垻.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *praːs): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 貝 (OC *paːds).
Related to Proto-Tai *paːᴮ (“grove; wood”), whence Thai ป่า (bpàa, “forest; meadow”), Lao ປ່າ (pā, “forest; wilderness”).

垼.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷeɡ): phonetic 役 (OC *ɢʷeɡ) + semantic 土 (“earth”).

埖.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 埖.

Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pʰrɯɡ): semantic 土 (“earth”) + phonetic 畐 (OC *pʰrɯɡ, *bɯɡ).

夂.e: 夂 is a component form of 止.

夃.e:
In oracle bone inscriptions, an ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 人 (“person”) + [Term?] (“mark on the thigh”) – thigh. Original form of 股 (gǔ).
Shuowen interprets its later form as an ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 𠄎 + 夂.

备.e: To go 夂 to the farm 田 to prepare for a harvest

変.e: To foment 攵 rebellion 䜌

复.e: A person 亻 who goes  somewhere 夂 every day 日

夏.e: Pictograph of a person, represented by their head (頁) and feet (夂), being heated underneath the sun (日). The sun component was later omitted for brevity during the Warring States period.

夔.e: A monster with a face, a nose 自, and one foot 夊

外.e: Night-time 夕 divinations 卜; the supernatural, the foreign

奂.e: Simplified form of 奐.  Origin unclear.

奡.e:
Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 𦣻 (“head”) + 大 (“person”). Original meaning unclear, possibly an arrogant person with their head held high, in which case the original form of 傲 (OC *ŋaːws, “arrogant”).
Bottom component is now written 夰 (OC *kuːʔ, *ɡuːʔ).
