斡.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʷaːd): phonetic 倝 (OC *kaːns) + semantic 斗.

斥.e: Someone scolded for cutting themselves 丶 with an axe 斤

斨.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰaŋ): phonetic 爿 (OC *braːn, *zaŋ) + semantic 斤 (“axe”)

断.e: Using an axe 斤 to harvest grain 米

斯.e: Phonosemantic compound. 斤 represents the meaning and 其 represents the sound. Nowadays this character is usually only used for its sound in phonetic loans.

新.e: A freshly chopped 斤 tree 亲

斲.e: To chop 斤 a tree trunk

斳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 堇 (OC *ɡrɯn, *kɯnʔ) + semantic 斤 (“axe”).

斵.e: To chop 斤 a tree trunk

斷.e: Threads 幺 severed 一 by an axe 斤

方.e: Origin unclear. Variously explained as a pictograph of a square tool, a part of a knife, or a raft.

於.e: Originally a variant form of 烏 (crow). The current use as a function word is a phonetic loan.

施.e: Phonosemantic compound. 㫃 represents the meaning and 也 represents the sound.

斿.e: Depicts a child (子) holding a flag (㫃).

旂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡɯl): semantic 㫃 + phonetic 斤 (OC *kɯn, *kɯns).

旄.e: A flag 方 made of fur 毛; 毛 also provides the pronunciation

旅.e: A man 方 traveling with a pack 氏 on his back

旋.e: An army 方 marching under a flag 疋

族.e: A group of people that swear by 矢 a single flag 方

旐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'ewʔ): semantic 㫃 + phonetic 兆 (OC *l'ewʔ).

旚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pʰew): semantic 㫃 + phonetic 票 (OC *pʰew)

旛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pʰan, *ban): semantic 㫃 (“flag”) + phonetic 番 (OC *paːl, *paːls, *pʰaːn, *pʰan, *ban)

旜.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tjan): semantic 㫃 + phonetic 亶 (OC *taːnʔ)

旝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *koːbs): semantic 㫃 + phonetic 會 (OC *koːbs, *ɡoːbs)

旞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ljuds): semantic 㫃 + phonetic 遂 (OC *ljuds).

旟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *la): semantic 㫃 + phonetic 與 (OC *la, *laʔ, *las)

旡.e: Pictograph of a person with their mouth facing backwards.

既.e: A person 旡 turning away from food 皀; compare 即

旦.e: The sun 日 rising over the horizon 一

旧.e: Older than 丨 the sun 日

旨.e: A spoon 匕 aimed for the mouth 日

早.e: The first rays 十 of the sun 日

旪.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 曰 (“to speak”) + 十 (“ten”) – many voices in harmony.

旬.e: A cycle 勹 of ten days 日; a traditional week

旱.e: The sun 日 baking a desert 干; 干 also provides the pronunciation

旳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 日 (“sun”) + phonetic 勺 (OC *pljewɢ, *bljewɢ). Later written as 的. Its original meaning is "bright".

旷.e: A wide 广 plain under the sun 日; 广 also provides the pronunciation

昃.e: The sun 日 at a slant 仄; 仄 also provides the pronunciation

昆.e: Two brothers 比 under the son 日

昇.e: The rising 升 sun 日; 升 also provides the pronunciation

昊.e: The sun 日 high in the sky 天

昌.e: Speaking 曰 in the daytime 日

明.e: The light of the sun 日 and moon 月

昏.e: Th sun 日 setting behind a village 氏

易.e: Upside-down version of 益 (increase; benefit), which depicts food or drink filling up a container (皿). Originally was the same character as 益, but the meaning later shifted to "easy".

昔.e: Twenty 廿 days ago 日

昙.e: The sun 日 hidden behind a cloud 云

昜.e: Bright and open, like the dawn 旦

昦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡuːʔ) and ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): semantic 日 (“sun”) + phonetic 夰 (OC *kuːʔ, *ɡuːʔ, “release”). Same as 昊 (OC *ɡuːʔ).

是.e: To speak 日 directly 疋

昶.e: A long 永 day 日; 永 also provides the pronunciation

昷.e: A warm 日 meal 皿

昼.e: When the sun is a foot 尺 above the horizon 旦

晅.e: To bake 火 in the sun 亘; 亘 also provides the pronunciation

晉.e: A platform 一 raised on stilts 厶

晋.e: An advance to the second 亚 day 日

晏.e: A woman safe in a house 安 at sunset 日

晔.e: A flower 华 blooming in the sun 日

晙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsluns, *sluns): semantic 日 (“sun”) + phonetic 夋 (OC *sʰlun).

晚.e: Avoiding 免 the sun 日; 免 also provides the pronunciation

晠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 日 + phonetic 成 (OC *djeŋ)

晧.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡuːʔ): semantic 日 (“white”) + phonetic 告 (OC *kuːɡs, *kuːɡ).

普.e: Everyone 並 under the sun 日

景.e: The sun 日 rising over a city 京; 京 also provides the pronunciation

晶.e: Many suns 日, suggesting a bright and glittering gem

智.e: Known 知 clearly 日; 知 also provides the pronunciation

暅.e:
* Eastern Min (BUC): òng
* Eastern Min
** (Fuzhou)
*** Bàng-uâ-cê: òng
*** Sinological IPA ⁽ᵏᵉʸ⁾: /ouŋ⁵³/

暋.e: Strong 敃 sunlight 日; 敃 also provides the pronunciation

暏.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 暏 – see 曙 (“daybreak; dawn”).
(This character is a variant form of 曙).

暐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷɯlʔ): semantic 日 (“day; sun”) + phonetic 韋 (OC *ɢʷɯl)

暘.e: Phonosemantic compound. 日 represents the meaning and 昜 represents the sound. Originally written as 昜.

暯.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 暯 – see 暮 (“evening; dusk; sunset; late; closing”).
(This character is an ancient form of 暮).

暴.e: A man with head 日 and hands 共 tormenting a deer 水

暵.e: Compound - the radical of the sun 日 and 堇 as a phonetic component; however, the phonetic component is linked to fire and heat since it represents a man burned alive.

暹.e: The sun 日 moving 辶; 隹 provides the pronunciation

曄.e: A flower 華 blooming in the sun 日

曇.e: The sun 日 hidden behind a cloud 雲

曌.e: Coined by Chinese empress regnant Wu Zetian. Wu Zetian chose 曌 (sometimes 瞾) as her own name after she became the empress regnant of China. 曌 and 瞾 both are variations of 照, and both were used during her regime only.

曏.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʰjaŋʔ, *qʰjaŋs, *qʰaŋʔ, *qʰaŋs): semantic 日 + phonetic 鄉 (OC *qʰaŋ).

曐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sleːŋ): semantic 晶 (“twinkling stars”) + phonetic 生 (OC *sʰleːŋ, *sreŋs).

曜.e: A spectacular 翟 star 日; 翟 also provides the pronunciation

曠.e: A wide 廣 plain under the sun 日; 廣 also provides the pronunciation

曰.e: Picture of an open 一 mouth 口

更.e: Phonosemantic compound. 攴 represents the meaning and 丙 represents the sound.

書.e: A brush 聿 used to write words 曰

曹.e: Depicts a pair of baskets (東). Based on the original meaning "pair". Later the meaning shifted to "of the same generation". The purpose of the additional 口 (mouth) component is unclear.

曼.e: Two hands 日, 又 pulling an eye open 罒

曾.e: A rice cooker on heat 日, building up steam 丷 and letting it off

替.e: One man 夫 replacing another; 日 provides the pronunciation

最.e: To take place 取 under the sun 日 (that is, everywhere)

朁.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰlɯːmʔ, *zlam): phonetic 兓 (OC *sl'ɯm) + semantic 口. The semantic component was originally 口 in oracle bone script, which later corrupted into 甘 in bronze script, then 曰 in seal script, and finally 日 in standard script.

有.e: Pictograph of a hand holding meat

服.e: A person 卩 putting on 又 a coat 月

朏.e:
Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 月 (“moon”) + 出 (“to come out; to appear”).
Perhaps from Austroasiatic; compare Khmer ពន្លូត (pɔɔpŭənluut, “to enlarge; to augment”, literally “to cause to appear; to grow”), from លូត (luut, “to sprout; to grow; to become longer”) (Schuessler, 2007).

朓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l̥ʰeːw, *l̥ʰeːwʔ): semantic 月 (“moon”) + phonetic 兆 (OC *l'ewʔ).

朕.e: Originally depicted two hands (廾) holding an object (丨) to patch up a hole in a boat (舟), representing the meaning "seam; crack". Since cracks in bones were used for fortune-telling in ancient China, the meaning shifted later to "omen; sign" and "sovereign".

朙.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 囧 (OC *kʷaŋʔ, “window”) + semantic 月 (“moon”) – the moon shining through a window – moonlight; bright. See 明 (OC *mraŋ) for other forms.

望.e: A king 王 gazing at the moon 月; 亡 provides the pronunciation

朞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kɯ): phonetic 其 (OC *kɯ, *ɡɯ) + semantic 月 (“moon”).

未.e: A wheat plant that has not yet borne fruit; compare 來

末.e: The top 一 of a tree 木

本.e: A tree 木 with its roots highlighted

术.e: Simplified form of 術.  Phonosemantic compound. 行 represents the meaning and 朮 represents the sound.  The traditional component 行 was removed for simplification.

朱.e: The color of the sap 一 of some trees 木

朳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *preːd): semantic 木 (“tree; wood”) + phonetic 八 (OC *preːd).

朵.e: Flowers 几 blooming on a tree 木

机.e: A table 几 made of wood 木; 几 also provides the pronunciation

朽.e: The wood of a dying 丂 tree 木

朾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rteːŋ, *rdeːŋ): semantic 木 + phonetic 丁 (OC *rteːŋ, *teːŋ).

杀.e: To kill 乂 someone and bury them in a coffin 木

杂.e: Simplified form of 雜.  Phonosemantic compound. 衣 represents the meaning and 集 represents the sound.

杅.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷa): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 于 (OC *ɢʷa). Compare 杇.

杇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʷaː): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 亏 (OC *ɢʷa). Compare 杅.

杈.e: Where two branches 木 fork 叉; 叉 also provides the pronunciation

杌.e: A tree 木 stump 兀; 兀 also provides the pronunciation

杏.e: A open mouth 口, waiting for fruit to fall from the tree 木

杒.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 杒 – see 軔 (“brake”).
(This character is an ancient form of 軔).

杓.e: A wooden 木 spoon 勺

杙.e: Phonosemantic compound. 木 represents the meaning and 弋 represents the sound. Originally written as 弋.

束.e: Lumber 木 bound by rope 口

杠.e: A wooden 木 lever 工; 工 also provides the pronunciation

条.e: Simplified form of 條.  Phonosemantic compound. 木 represents the meaning and 攸 represents the sound.

来.e: A wheat plant that has not yet borne fruit; compare 來

杪.e: The tip 少 of a branch 木; 少 also provides the pronunciation

杰.e: Simplified form of 傑. Phonosemantic compound. 亻 represents the meaning and 桀 represents the sound.

東.e: The sun 日 rising behind a tree 木; compare 西

杲.e: The sun 日 rising over the woods 木

杳.e: The sun 日 setting below the woods 木

杻.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *nuʔ, *n̥ʰuʔ): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 丑 (OC *n̥ʰuʔ) – wooden manacles or handcuffs. However, the phonetic component indicates either a hand or an animal claw, hence it adds part of the meaning.

枅.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kŋeː): semantic 木 (“wood”) + phonetic 幵 (OC *kŋeːn)

枊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋaːŋ, *ŋaːŋs): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 卬 (OC *ŋaːŋ, *ŋaŋʔ).

枋.e: A square 方 log 木; 方 also provides the pronunciation

析.e: Chopping 斤 wood 木; 斤 also provides the pronunciation

林.e: Two trees 木 representing a forest

枙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 厄 (OC *qreːɡ).

果.e: Fruit 田 growing on a tree 木

枣.e: Dates ⺀ falling off a tree 朿

枭.e: A bird 鸟 roosting in a tree 木

枯.e: An old 古 tree 木; 古 also provides the pronunciation

枰.e: A flat 平 piece of wood 木; 平 also provides the pronunciation

枱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 (“wood”) + phonetic 台 (OC *l̥ʰɯː, *l'ɯː, *l'ɯː, *lɯ).

枹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pruː, *bu, *bu): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 包 (OC *pruː).

柊.e: A winter 冬 tree 木; 冬 also provides the pronunciation

某.e: The sweet 甘 fruit of a certain tree 木

柑.e: A tree 木 bearing sweet fruit 甘; 甘 also provides the pronunciation

柒.e: Seven 七 with accents to prevent forgery

染.e: Water 氵 and wine 九 mixed in a wooden 木 bowl

柔.e: Wood 木 so soft it can be cut 矛

查.e: Phonosemantic compound. 木 represents the meaning and 且 represents the sound. Based on the original meaning "hawthorn tree". The meaning "check; look up" is a phonetic loan.

柩.e: A wooden 木 coffin 匛; 匛 also provides the pronunciation

柭.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 柭 – see 柫 (“flail; to strike; to hit; to beat; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 柫).

柲.e: Phonosemantic compound. 木 represents the meaning and 必 represents the sound. Originally written as 必, a pictograph of a weapon.

柵.e: A fence 冊 made of wood 木; 冊 also provides the pronunciation

査.e: Phonosemantic compound. 木 represents the meaning and 且 represents the sound. Based on the original meaning "hawthorn tree". The meaning "check; look up" is a phonetic loan.

栃.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 栃 (tochi).
Spelling pronunciation, as 勵／励 (lì) or 蠣／蛎 (lì).

栅.e: A fence 册 made of wood 木; 册 also provides the pronunciation

栉.e: A wooden 木 comb 节; 节 also provides the pronunciation

栔.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰeːds): phonetic 㓞 (qià) + semantic 木. However, the phonetic components represents some incisions made with a knife.
Possibly a variant of 契 or vice versa, given that the two characters were used interchangeably in ancient text.

栗.e: A tree 木 bearing nuts 覀; compare 果

栮.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 (“tree; wood”) + phonetic 耳 (OC *njɯʔ).
From 耳 (OC *njɯʔ, “ear”).

桌.e: Phonosemantic compound. 木 represents the meaning and 卓 represents the sound.

桑.e: A tree 木 bearing berries 叒

桖.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 (“wood”) + phonetic 血 (“blood”).

桙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 牟 (OC *mu).

桮.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pɯː): semantic 木 + phonetic 否 (OC *brɯʔ, *pɯʔ).

桸.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hral, *hlɯl): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 希 (OC *hlɯl).

桺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 + phonetic 丣 (OC *luʔ).

桼.e: A tree 木 whose sap 氺 is used as varnish

梁.e: A wooden 木 bridge built 刅 over a river 氵

梂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡu): semantic 木 (“wood”) + phonetic 求 (OC *ɡu)

條.e: A person 亻 counting 丨; 条 provides the pronunciation

梟.e: A bird 鳥 roosting in a tree 木

梦.e: Two eyes shut 林 at night 夕

梹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 + phonetic 兵 (OC *praŋ).

棄.e: Pictograph of two hands (廾) putting a child (子) in a basket (其) for the purpose of abandoning or disposing with it.

棆.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *run): semantic 木 (“wood”) + phonetic 侖 (OC *run).

棉.e: Silk 帛 picked off a tree 木

棑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *breː, *brɯːls): semantic 木 (“wood”) + phonetic 非 (OC *pɯl).

棓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *bɯ, *broːŋʔ, *bɯː): semantic 木 (“tree, wood”) + phonetic 咅 (OC *pʰɯʔ, *pʰl'oːs).

棗.e: A tree 朿 with many thorns 朿

棡.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 岡 (OC *klaːŋ).

棢.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *maŋʔ): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 罔 (OC *mlaŋʔ).

棥.e: Forests 木 separated by a fence 爻

森.e: Many trees 木 forming a forest

椄.e: From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *sap (“wedge”); compare Burmese သပ် (sap, “to whittle down; wedge”) (STEDT).

椏.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qaːʔ, *qraː): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 亞 (OC *qraːɡs).

椑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *beː, *pe, *beɡ, *beːɡ): semantic 木 (“tree; wood”) + phonetic 卑 (OC *pe).

椗.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 椗 – see 碇 (“anchor; to anchor; to berth; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 碇).

椛.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 椛.
Spelling pronunciation, as 花 (huā).

椶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsoːŋ): semantic 木 + phonetic 㚇 (zōng).

楘.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *moːɡ): phonetic 敄 (OC *moʔ, *mos) + semantic 木 (“wood”).

楚.e: Phonosemantic compound. 林 represents the meaning and 疋 represents the sound. Based on the original meaning "bush". The meaning later shifted to "distinct" and "clear".

楛.e: A bitter 苦 plant 木; 苦 also provides the pronunciation

業.e: A musical instrument 业 made of wood 木

楷.e: The script 木 used by commoners 皆

楽.e: Unorthodox variant simplified from 樂 (𢆶 (yōu) → ⿱丷八) found in the Ming dynasty orthographic dictionary 《字學三正》. Also found in 《宋元以來俗字譜》, a variant forms dictionary compiled in 1930 that records unorthodox forms (俗字 (súzì)) that have existed since the Song dynasty.

榊.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 榊 (sakaki).
Spelling pronunciation, as 神 (shén).

榑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ba): semantic 木 (“wood”) + phonetic 尃 (OC *pʰa).

榘.e: A wooden 木 carpenter's square 矩; 矩 also provides the pronunciation

榜.e: A sign posted 旁 on a tree 木; 旁 also provides the pronunciation

榡.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 + phonetic 素 (OC *saːs).

榪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mraːs): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 馬 (OC *mraːʔ).

榮.e: Grass 炏 and trees 木 flourishing in a garden 冖

榻.e: A bed of wood 木 and feathers 羽 to sleep in at night 日

槪.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 㮣 – see 概 (“general idea; general; rough; approximate; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 概).

槯.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zluːl): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 崔 (OC *zluːl, *sʰluːl).

槷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋeːd): phonetic 埶 (OC *ŋeds) + semantic 木 (“wood”)

樂.e: A musical instrument with wood 木, strings 幺, and a pick 白

樑.e: A wooden 木 beam 梁; 梁 also provides the pronunciation

樛.e: Branches 木 bowing in the wind 翏; 翏 also provides the pronunciation

樴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *dɯːɡ, *tjɯɡ): semantic 木 (“wood”) + phonetic 戠 (OC *tjɯɡ).

樵.e: Wood 木 for burning 焦; 焦 also provides the pronunciation

樷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 林 (“forest”) + phonetic 取 (OC *sʰloːʔ, *sʰloʔ).

樽.e: A hand 寸 raising a wooden 木 wine jar 酉; 尊 also provides the pronunciation

橆.e: Originally a pictograph of a person (大) dancing with ornamental leather hides (革) hanging from his arms, conveying the meaning "dance". Later, two feet (舛) were added to the bottom of the character to form 舞 (dance), and 無 was borrowed for its sound to represent the meaning "not".

橔.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 橔 – see 𧝋 (“cover for a coffin”).
(This character is a variant form of 𧝋).

機.e: A table 幾 made of wood 木; 幾 also provides the pronunciation

橤.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 惢 (OC *sqʰloːlʔ, *sklol, *sɡlolʔ) + semantic 木 (“tree”).

橮.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 木 + phonetic 貿 (OC *mlus).

橱.e: A wooden 木 cabinet 厨; 厨 also provides the pronunciation

毑.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 毑 – see 姐 (“elder sister; woman; lady; madame; etc.”).
(This character is recorded in one or more historical dictionaries as an ancient form of 姐).

Attested in Lingwai Daida (1178) among local characters used in Guangxi: 「氼，音魅，言沒人在水下也。」
Related to Hokkien 沬 (bī), Eastern Min 沬 (mê), Hakka 沕 (mi⁴), Xiang 沕 (mei⁵) (note that they all have tone 陽去).

; Etymology 4
Simplified from 洑 (伏 → 犬).

漙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *doːn): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 專 (OC *tjon)

漦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sɦrɯ): phonetic 𠩺 + semantic 水 (“water”).

漩.e: Swirling 旋 water 氵; 旋 also provides the pronunciation

漱.e: To gargle 欶 water 氵; 欶 also provides the pronunciation

漼.e: A deep 崔 pool 氵; 崔 also provides the pronunciation

潀.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 潀 – see 潨 (“to flow into a larger river; confluence; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 潨).

潁.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷeŋʔ): semantic 水 (“river”) + phonetic 頃 (OC *kʰʷeŋ, *kʰʷeŋʔ).

潆.e: Water 氵 coiling 萦 on itself; 萦 also provides the pronunciation

潐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsews): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 焦 (OC *ʔsew).

潑.e: To send forth 發 water 氵

潒.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 潒 – see 蕩 (“to sway; to swing; to wash away; to cleanse; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 蕩).

潚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sɯːw, *sɯwɢ): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 肅 (OC *sɯwɢ).

潨.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zloːŋ, *zluːŋ, *tjuŋ): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 衆 (OC *tjuŋ, *tjuŋs).

潩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *lɯɡs, *lɯɡ): semantic 氵 (“water”) + phonetic 異 (OC *lɯɡs).

潬.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 潬 – see 灘 (“shoal; beach; etc.”).
(This character is an ancient form of 灘).

潲.e: A little 稍 water 氵; 稍 also provides the pronunciation

潸.e: Water 氵 falling from closed eyes 林

澀.e: Water 氵 that cuts like a knife 歰; 歰 also provides the pronunciation

澁.e: Water 氵 that cuts like a knife 歰; 歰 also provides the pronunciation

澍.e: Rain 氵 that saves 尌 the farm; 尌 also provides the pronunciation

澐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢun): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 雲 (OC *ɢun).

澗.e: A stream 氵 flowing through a valley 間; 間 also provides the pronunciation

澛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 魯 (OC *raːʔ).

澴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡʷraːn): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 睘 (OC *ɡʷeŋ)

濁.e: A pond 氵 growing scum 蜀

濄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kloːl, *qloːl): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 過 (OC *kloːl, *kloːls).

濇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *srɯb, *srɯɡ): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 嗇 (OC *srɯɡ).

濘.e: Still 寧 water 氵; 寧 also provides the pronunciation

濚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 氵 (“water”) + phonetic 榮 (OC *ɢʷreŋ).

濼.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pqʰlaːwɢ, *raːwɢ, *reːwɢ, *pqʰloːwɢ, *roːwɢ, *roːwɢ): semantic 氵 (“water”) + phonetic 樂 (OC *ŋraːwɢs, *raːwɢ, *ŋraːwɢ).

濿.e: From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *rap (“to cross (river), to ford, to wade”) (provisional) (STEDT). Cognate with Tibetan རབ (rab, “ford”) & Jingpho [script needed] (rap⁵⁵); possibly unrelated to 瀨 (OC *raːds) (Schuessler, 2007).

瀂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 魯 (OC *raːʔ).

瀅.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʷeːŋs): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 瑩 (OC *ɢʷreŋ, *qʷeːŋs)

瀍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *dan): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 廛 (OC *dan)

瀑.e: Violent 暴 water 氵; 暴 also provides the pronunciation

瀒.e: Water 氵 that cuts like a knife 歰; 歰 also provides the pronunciation

瀔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *koːɡ): semantic 氵 (“water”) + phonetic 穀 (OC *kloːɡ).

瀤.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡruːl): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 褱 (OC *ɡruːl, *ɡruːl)

瀦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ta): semantic 水 + phonetic 豬 (OC *ta).

瀰.e: Water 氵 everywhere 彌; 彌 also provides the pronunciation

灇.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 灇 – see 潨 (“to flow into a larger river; confluence; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 潨).

灈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡʷa): semantic 氵 (“water; river”) + phonetic 瞿 (OC *kʷas, *ɡʷa).

灕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rel): semantic 水 (“water”) + phonetic 離 (OC *reːls, *rel, *rels).

灘.e: Difficult 難 waters 氵 to navigate; 難 also provides the pronunciation

灠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡ·raːmʔ): semantic 氵 (“water”) + phonetic 覽 (OC *ɡ·raːmʔ).

灩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *lams): semantic 氵 + phonetic 豔 (OC *lams).

灬.e: 灬 is a component form of 火.

灭.e: To cover 一 a flame 火

灰.e: The remains of a fire 火; 火 also provides the pronunciation

灶.e: A clay 土 oven 火

灷.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 火 (“fire”) + 廾 (“hands”) – fire that can be handled, embers, hot coals.

災.e: A river 巛 on fire 火

灾.e: A house 宀 on fire 火

炁.e: Found in various historical rime dictionaries such as Guangyun 《廣韻》 and Jiyun 《集韻》.

炅.e: A flame 火 as bright as the sun 日

炉.e: A fire 火 in one's home 户

炏.e: Two blades of grass; compare 艸

炪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kljod, *l̥ʰud): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 出 (OC *kʰljuds, *kʰljud).

炷.e: A lit 火 candle 主; 主 also provides the pronunciation

炸.e: A sudden 乍 flame 火; 乍 also provides the pronunciation

為.e: A hand 爫 leading an elephant

炻.e: Fired 火 clay 石; 石 also provides the pronunciation

烁.e: The happy 乐 light of a flame 火

烄.e: Related to Etymology 1, though the details are unclear (STEDT).

烐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 舟 (OC *tjɯw).

烕.e: To smother 戊 a flame 灭; 灭 also provides the pronunciation

烖.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔslɯː): semantic 火 + phonetic 𢦏 (OC *ʔslɯː, “disaster”); a form of 𢦏 with 火 added, the form used in the seal script.

烜.e: To bake 火 in the sun 亘; 亘 also provides the pronunciation

烨.e: A glorious 华 blaze 火; 华 also provides the pronunciation

烬.e: A used up 尽 fire 火; 尽 also provides the pronunciation

焀.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡloːɡ): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 谷 (OC *ɦkroːɡ, *kloːɡ, *ɡ·loːɡ)

焉.e: A bird 鳥 with a strange head

焋.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 焋 – see 𣴣 (“to fill a cauldron or rice pot with rice”).
(This character is an ancient form of 𣴣).

焚.e: Setting fire 火 to a forest 林

焜.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡuːnʔ): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 昆 (OC *kuːn).

無.e: Originally a pictograph of a person (大) dancing with ornamental leather hides (革) hanging from his arms, conveying the meaning "dance". Later, two feet (舛) were added to the bottom of the character to form 舞 (dance), and 無 was borrowed for its sound to represent the meaning "not".

焦.e: A bird 隹 getting its tail singed 灬

焫.e: Compare Zhuang ndat (“hot”), Thai เดือด (dʉ̀ʉat, “to boil”).

煑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tjaʔ): phonetic 者 (OC *tjaːʔ) + semantic 火 (“fire”) – to cook over a fire.

煓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tʰoːn): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 耑 (OC *toːn).

煕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 巸 (OC *lɯ) + semantic 灬 (“fire”)

煜.e: A dazzling 昱 flame 火; 昱 also provides the pronunciation

煠.e: A sudden 乍 flame 火; 乍 also provides the pronunciation

煦.e: A warm 昫 fire 灬; 昫 also provides the pronunciation

煳.e: A reckless 胡 flame 火; 胡 also provides the pronunciation

煴.e:
An unorthodox variant simplified from 熅 (𥁕 → 昷) which can be found in various historical dictionaries.
Eventually adopted as an official form by the People's Republic of China in 1965 based on the Xin Zixing (新字形) standardized form of characters.

煺.e: To prepare 退 to cook 火; 退 also provides the pronunciation

煽.e: To fan 扇 the flames 火; 扇 also provides the pronunciation

熄.e: To end 息 a flame 火; 息 also provides the pronunciation

熊.e: A bear's 能 footprints 灬

熔.e: To form 容 hot metal 火; 容 also provides the pronunciation

熕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 貢 (OC *koːŋs) + semantic 火.

熤.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 火 + phonetic 翌 (OC *lɯɡ).

熲.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʷeːŋʔ): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 頃 (OC *kʰʷeŋ, *kʰʷeŋʔ)
Nominal of 熒 (OC *ɡʷeːŋ) with prefix k-. See there for further etymology.

燂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'ɯːm, *zlom, *ljum): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 覃 (OC *l'ɯːm).

燄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *lomʔ): phonetic 臽 (OC *kʰloːmʔ, *ɡroːms) + semantic 炎.

燇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 尊 (OC *ʔsuːn)

燔.e: To roast 火 meat on a spit 番; 番 also provides the pronunciation

燕.e: A swallow, with its head 廿, wings 北, and tail 灬

營.e: Phonosemantic compound. 宮 represents the meaning and 熒 represents the sound.

燫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡ·reːm): semantic 火 + phonetic 廉 (OC *ɡ·rem).

燬.e: Damage 毁 from fire 火; 毁 also provides the pronunciation

燻.e: Smoke 熏 from a fire 火; 熏 also provides the pronunciation

燼.e: A used up 盡 fire 火; 盡 also provides the pronunciation

爁.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡ·raːmʔ, *ɡ·raːms, *ɡ·rams): semantic 火 (“fire”) + phonetic 監 (OC *kraːm, *kraːms)

爆.e: A violent 暴 flame 火; 暴 also provides the pronunciation

爍.e: The happy 樂 light of a flame 火

爐.e: A fire 火 in one's home 盧

爒.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rewʔ, *reːwʔ): semantic 炙 (“burn”) + phonetic 尞.

爖.e: Compare Burmese လောင် (laung, “to burn, scorch”) (Luce, 1981).

爚.e: Probably related to 曜 (OC *lewɢs, “to shine; daylight”); see there for more (STEDT).

爭.e: Two hands 爪 and 彐 fighting over 亅

爯.e: A hand 爫 balancing items on a scale 冉

爰.e: Pictograph of two hands pulling a rope.

爱.e: To bring a friend 友 into one's house 冖

爵.e: A hand 寸 holding a goblet 良 of wine 罒

父.e: A hand 乂 holding an axe 八

爻.e: Two marks 乂 on an oracle bone

爽.e: A person 大 breathing lots of fresh air 爻

爾.e: Origin unclear. Used in ancient texts to mean "you".

爿.e: Half of a tree trunk; compare 片

牀.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zraŋ): phonetic 爿 (OC *braːn, *zaŋ, “bed”) + semantic 木 (“wood”). 爿 originally referred to this word, 木 was added later.

片.e: Half of a tree trunk; compare 爿

牒.e: Flat 片 pages 片 of wood

牕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 穴 + phonetic 悤 (cōng).

牚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rtʰaːŋs): phonetic 尚 (OC *djaŋ, *djaŋs) + semantic 牙

牠.e:
; it (all non-human objects)
In Qian Xuantong's discussion on the creation of new vernacular pronouns, advocates for this form intended to evoke the left radical of 物 (wù, “thing”) rather than having the semantic implications of 牛 (niú, “bovine”).

牢.e: A stable 宀 where cattle are kept 牛

牦.e: The fur 毛 of an ox 牛; 毛 also provides the pronunciation

牧.e: A hand 攵 leading an ox 牛

牵.e: A person 大 pulling an ox 牛 by a rope 冖

牽.e: A person pulling 冖 an ox 牛 by a rope 玄

犀.e: An ox 牛 with a tail 尾

犁.e: To use an ox 牛 to harvest 刂 grain 禾; 利 also provides the pronunciation

犂.e: To use an ox 牛 to harvest  grain 禾; compare 犁

犊.e: To sacrifice 卖 a calf 牛

犢.e: To sacrifice 賣 a calf 牛

犬.e: A dog turned to the side

犭.e: A dog turned to the side

犮.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 犬 (“dog”) + 丿. According to Shuowen Jiezi, it represents a walking dog.

犵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 乞 (OC *kʰɯds, *kʰɯd)

犼.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犭 (“canine”) + phonetic 孔 (OC *kʰloːŋʔ)

狑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *reːŋ): semantic 犭 + phonetic 令 (OC *ren, *reŋ, *reŋs, *reːŋ, *reːŋs).

狝.e:
Alternative forms with 𤣔. Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *smlenʔ): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 爾 (OC *njelʔ).
Etymology 1: Simplified from 𤣗 (璽 → 爾).
Etymology 2: Simplified from 獼 (彌 → 爾).

狦.e: Shuowen: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 刪 (OC *sraːn)

狫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 老 (OC *ruːʔ) (see also Graphic pejoratives in written Chinese#Dog radical)

狱.e: A guard dog 犭 talking 讠 to a prisoner 犬

狵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mroːŋ): semantic 犭 (“dog”) + phonetic 尨 (OC *mroːŋ).

狶.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 狶 – see 豨 (“pig; hog; swine”).
(This character is a variant form of 豨).

狾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔljeds): semantic 犭 (“dog; canine; beast”) + phonetic 折 (OC *l'eːl, *ʔljed, *ɦljed).

猋.e: A pack of ferocious dogs 犬; a storm

猒.e:
In bronze inscriptions, ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): a dog 犬 eating 口 (“mouth”) meat ⺼ – "satisfied".
In the modern form, 口 has corrupted into 甘 and later 日.

猚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋreːl, *rulʔ): phonetic 犬 (OC *kʰʷeːnʔ) + semantic 隹 (“bird”)

猣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsoːŋ, *ʔsoːŋʔ): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 㚇 (zōng).

獁.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犭 + phonetic 馬 (OC *mraːʔ).

獃.e: One with wooden 木 speech 口

獄.e: A guard dog 犭 talking 言 to a prisoner 犬

獋.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 獋 – see 嗥 (“to howl; to wail”).
(This character is a variant form of 嗥).

獑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zeːm, *zaːm): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 斬 (OC *ʔsreːmʔ).

獚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡʷaːŋ): semantic 犭 (“dog”) + phonetic 黃 (OC *ɡʷaːŋ).

獛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *poːɡ): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 菐 (OC *boːɡ, *puɡ)

獜.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rin, *reːŋ): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 粦 (OC *rins)

獞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 犭 (“dog”) + phonetic 童 (OC *doːŋ).

獟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋeːws): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 堯 (OC *ŋeːw)

獠.e: Hunting with dogs 犭 and torches 尞; 尞 also provides the pronunciation

獩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʷads): semantic 犭 (“dog”) + phonetic 歲 (OC *sqʰʷads, *sqʰʷad).

獮.e:
Alternative forms with 𤣔. Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *smlenʔ): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 爾 (OC *njelʔ).
Etymology 1: Simplified from 𤣗 (璽 → 爾).
Etymology 2: Simplified from 獼 (彌 → 爾).

獶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qu): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 憂 (OC *qu).

獹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *raː): semantic 犬 (“dog”) + phonetic 盧 (OC *b·raː)

率.e: A string 幺 vibrating in an instrument 亠十

玈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *raː): semantic 玄 (“deep, mysterious”) + abbreviated phonetic 旅 (OC *ɡ·raʔ).

玉.e: A necklace 丨 adorned with three pieces of jade

王.e: A man 十 bridging heaven and earth (both 一)

玠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kreːds): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 介 (OC *kreːds).

玨.e: Two guitar strings bound across frets

现.e: To see 见 jade 王; 见 also provides the pronunciation

珀.e: Clear 白 jade 王; 白 also provides the pronunciation

珂.e: Possibly 可 jade 王; 可 also provides the pronunciation

珈.e: Jade 王 added 加 to jewelry; 加 also provides the pronunciation

珏.e: Two pieces of jade 王 side by side

珓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kreːws): semantic 𤣩 (“jade”) + phonetic 交 (OC *kreːw).

珔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 𤣩 + phonetic 存 (OC *zluːn).

珕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *reːbs, *rebs): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 劦 (OC *ɦleːb).

珥.e: An ear 耳 ring 王; 耳 also provides the pronunciation

珦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hljaŋs, *hlaŋs): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 向 (OC *hljaŋs, *hlaŋs).

珪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʷeː): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 圭 (OC *kʷeː).

珬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *smid): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 戌 (OC *smid).

班.e: Cutting 刂 a piece of jade 王 in two, symbolizing two classes

珺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 𤣩 (“jade”) + phonetic 君 (OC *klun).

現.e: To see 見 jade 王; 見 also provides the pronunciation

琇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *luʔ, *slus): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 秀 (OC *slus).

琈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *bu): semantic 𤣩 (“jade”) + phonetic 孚 (OC *pʰuw).

琥.e: A tiger's eye 虎 stone 王; 虎 also provides the pronunciation

琭.e: From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *r-lu(ŋ/k) (“stone”); possibly related to 玉 (OC *ŋoɡ) and 碭 (OC *l'aːŋ) (STEDT).

琯.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *koːnʔ, *kuːns): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 官 (OC *koːn).

琰.e: The fire 炎 in a gem 王; 炎 also provides the pronunciation

琱.e: Phonosemantic compound. 𤣩 represents the meaning and 周 represents the sound.

琲.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *buːlʔ): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 非 (OC *pɯl).

瑊.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *krɯːm, *kjum): semantic 玉 (“jade”) + phonetic 咸 (OC *ɡrɯːm).

璯.e:
Characters in the same phonetic series (會) (Zhengzhang, 2003)

甀.e: Sino-Tibetan? Compare Tibetan ཡོལ་གོ (yol go) and ཡོལ་མ (yol ma) "earthenware, crockery" (Schuessler, 2007)

甜.e: Something tasty 甘 to the tongue 舌

甭.e: Not 不 needed 用; 用 also provides the pronunciation

电.e: Simplified form of 電; lightning 申 from a storm cloud 雨

男.e: Someone who can work 力 the farm 田

画.e: A painting on an easel; compare 畫, which includes the brush

甾.e: A farm 田 wiped out by a flood 巛

畄.e: Phonosemantic compound. 田 represents the meaning and 卯 represents the sound.

畆.e: A man 久 pacing out a plot of land 亩

畋.e: A man 攵 working on a farm 田; 田 also provides the pronunciation

畏.e: A monster with a scary head 田

畐.e: Pictograph of a container full of something.

畑.e: A dry 火 field 田; 田 also provides the pronunciation

畔.e: A farm 田 divided in half 半; 半 also provides the pronunciation

留.e: Phonosemantic compound. 田 represents the meaning and 卯 represents the sound.

畝.e: A man 久 pacing out a plot of land 亩

畟.e: A man 夂 making a furrow 八 in a field 田

Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 畠 (hatake).
Spelling pronunciation, as 田 (tián, “field”).

畬.e: An extra 余 field 田; 佘 provides the pronunciation; compare 畲

畱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 丣 (OC *luʔ) + semantic 田 (“field”)

畲.e: An extra 余 field 田; 佘 provides the pronunciation; compare 畬

畵.e: To paint 聿 a canvas 田 on an easel 一

畸.e: Leftover 奇 farmland 田; 奇 also provides the pronunciation

畺.e: Lines 一 drawn between two plots of land 田

畾.e: Three fields 田 divided by dikes

疊.e: Clothes 畾 stacked on top of a drawer 冝

疋.e: A foot 止 with a leg on top

疎.e: Walking 疋 through uncultivated land 㐬

疏.e: Walking 疋 through uncultivated land 㐬

疑.e: An old man walking 疋 while holding 匕 a cane 矢

瘈.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“illness; disease”) + phonetic 契 (OC *kʰeːds, *kʰeːd, *kʰeːd, *kʰɯd).
Sagart (2021c) revises the Old Chinese reconstruction as *k(ə)-tet-s, from non-finite prefix *k(ə)- + a root *tet-s (“to bite”), from which 噬 (OC *m-tet-s, “to bite”) also derives.

瘍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *laŋ): semantic 疒 (“illness”) + phonetic 昜 (OC *laŋ).

瘞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qrebs): phonetic 㾜 + semantic 土 (“earth”).

癐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“sick; ill”) + phonetic 會 (OC *koːbs, *ɡoːbs).

癦.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 疒 (“illness”) + phonetic 墨.
From 墨 (mak6, “ink”).

皀.e: Pictograph of a food vessel for holding grain.

皋.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 白 (“white, bright”) + 夲 (“to advance”), although the connection to the modern meaning is unclear.

皪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *reːwɢ): semantic 白 + phonetic 樂 (OC *ŋraːwɢs, *raːwɢ, *ŋraːwɢ).

皻.e: Spots 虘 on the skin 皮; 虘 also provides the pronunciation

盪.e: Hot soup 湯 in a bowl 皿; 湯 also provides the pronunciation

For pronunciation and definitions of 盽 – see 䀱 (“examine, observe, to survey, to study”).
(This character is a variant form of 䀱).

瞾.e: Coined by Chinese empress regnant Wu Zetian. Wu Zetian chose 曌 (sometimes 瞾) as her own name after she became the empress regnant of China. 曌 and 瞾 both are variations of 照, and both were used during her regime only.

矕.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mroːnʔ): phonetic 䜌 (OC *b·roːn, *b·ron, *b·rons) + semantic 目 (“eye”).
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *mV-taŋ (“to look, to see”).

矛.e: A spear pointed up and to the right

矦.e: An arrow 矢 hitting a target 厃

石.e: A rock 口 at the base of a cliff 厂

砀.e: A bright 昜 stone 石; 昜 also provides the pronunciation

砌.e: To cut 切 stone 石; 切 also provides the pronunciation

砑.e: Stone 石 used as teeth 牙; 牙 also provides the pronunciation

砛.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 砛 – see 玪 (“jadestone similar to”).
(This character is a variant form of 玪).

砬.e: A large standing 立 stone 石; 立 also provides the pronunciation

砱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *reːŋ): semantic 石 (“stone”) + phonetic 令 (OC *ren, *reŋ, *reŋs, *reːŋ, *reːŋs).

Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 硴.

碧.e: A stone 石 like amber 珀; 珀 also provides the pronunciation

碭.e: Possibly related to 玉 (OC *ŋoɡ, “jade”); see there for more (STEDT).

磒.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢunʔ): semantic 石 (“stone”) + phonetic 員 (OC *ɢon, *ɢun).

磪.e: A bird 隹 faced with a mountain 山

磬.e: Stone 石 chimes 殸; 殸 also provides the pronunciation

礜.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *las): phonetic 與 (OC *la, *laʔ, *las) + semantic 石 (“stone; rock”)

礩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tjids, *tjid): semantic 石 (“stone; rock”) + phonetic 質 (OC *tids, *tjid).

礫.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *reːwɢ): semantic 石 (“stone”) + phonetic 樂 (OC *ŋraːwɢs, *raːwɢ, *ŋraːwɢ)
From Proto-Tibeto-Burman *b-rak. Cognate with Drung ɑ³¹ pɹɑʔ⁵⁵ luŋ⁵⁵ (“rock”)

礵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 石 (“stone”) + phonetic 霜 (OC *sraŋ).

礻.e: 礻 is a component form of 示.

祐.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷɯs): semantic 礻 (“spirit”) + phonetic 右 (OC *ɢʷɯʔ, *ɢʷɯs).
Exoactive of 右 (OC *ɢʷɯʔ, *ɢʷɯs, “right (side)”) (Schuessler, 2007).

禸.e:
According to Xu Shen, it is the pictogram of a print of an animal paw.
Unrelated to the bottom part of 禹, 离, 禽, and 禺. Also unrelated to 虫, in which it represents the tail of a snake, and 萬, in which it represents the tail of a scorpion.

禹.e: Depicts Yu the Great (21st century BC), a legendary leader who tamed the floods.

禺.e: Remnant of 禹, a legendary leader named Yu.

羅.e: A silk 糹 net 罒 for catching birds 隹

羈.e: A leather 革 halter used to control 罒a horse  馬

美.e: A person 大 wearing an elegant crown 羊

羙.e: A person 大 wearing an elegant crown 羊

Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *daʔ): semantic 羊 (“sheep”) + phonetic 宁 (OC *da, *daʔ)
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *ra (“goat”) (STEDT). Cognate with Tibetan ར (ra, “goat”), Pattani lá (“goat”), etc.

For the organic compound, Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 羊 (“oxygen, from 氧”) + 夋 (“acid, from 酸”).
Blend of 氧 (yǎng, “oxygen”) + 酸 (suān, “acid”) and borrowed from English carboxyl.

Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *lo): semantic 羊 (“sheep; goat”) + phonetic 俞 (OC *l̥ʰus, *lo).
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *luk (“sheep”) (STEDT). Probably cognate with 犢 (OC *l'oːɡ, “calf”) (Schuessler, 2007; STEDT).

羶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hljan): semantic 羊 + phonetic 亶 (OC *taːnʔ).

Related to 翂 (OC *pɯn, “appearance of birds flying slowly”) and 飛 (OC *pɯl, “to fly”).

翆.e: Unorthodox variant of 翠 (cuì). The phonetic component 卆 is a variant of 卒.

翌.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *lɯɡ): phonetic 羽 (OC *ɢʷaʔ, *ɢʷas) + semantic 昱

翾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʰʷen): phonetic 睘 (OC *ɡʷeŋ) + semantic 羽 (“wings”)

Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *nɯːs): phonetic 而 (OC *njɯ) + semantic 寸.
STEDT compares this to 難 (OC *n̥ʰaːn, *naːns) and Proto-Tibeto-Burman *na (“ill, pain, sore, ache, difficult, evil spirit”)

耺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢun): semantic 耳 (“ear”) + phonetic 云 (OC *ɢun).

聂.e: A pair 双 of ears 耳

聒.e: The sound 耳 of a bell 舌

聖.e: A ruler 王 who speaks 口 and listens 耳

聚.e: People standing side-by-side 乑, hand-to-ear 取

聬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 耳 (“ear”) + phonetic 翁 (OC *qloːŋ).

肘.e: A arm carrying an object 寸 at the side of the body ⺼

肙.e: A head 口 on a long body ⺼

脉.e: Long 永 vessels through one's body ⺼

自.e: A nose; in China, people refer to themselves by pointing to their noses

臬.e: A notice 自 posted on a tree 木

臭.e: The smell 自 of a dirty dog 犬

臮.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡrids): phonetic 自 (OC *ɦljids) + semantic 乑 (“three men”).

臯.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 白 (“white, bright”) + 夲 (“to advance”), although the connection to the modern meaning is unclear.

至.e: A bird alighting on the ground 土

臺.e: A man 吉 standing on a raised 至 dais 冖

臼.e: A mortar used to grind out powders

臽.e: A person ⺈ falling in a pit 臼

臾.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 𦥑 (“two hands”) + 人 (“person”) – two hands dragging a person. Compare 曳.

臿.e: A mortar 臼 and pestle 千

舀.e: A hand 爫 fetching food from a container 臼

舁.e: Two hands 廾 lifting a mortar 臼

舆.e: A cart 车 carried on one's shoulder 舁

與.e: Phonosemantic compound. 舁 represents the meaning and 牙 represents the sound.

舊.e: Phonosemantic compound. 雈 represents the meaning and 臼 represents the sound. Based on the original meaning "type of owl". The current meaning is a phonetic loan.

舋.e: A sacrifice made on an altar 且

舌.e: A tongue 千 sticking out of a mouth 口

舓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɦljeʔ): semantic 舌 + phonetic 易 (OC *leːɡs, *leɡ).

舛.e: Pictograph of two feet facing opposite directions.

舞.e: A hand 舛 holding a fan

舦.e: Vernacular reading of 舵 (Norman, 1988, Yan et al., 2016).

般.e: Using a tool 殳 to sort items on a tray 舟

舱.e: A ship's 舟 cabin 仓; 仓 also provides the pronunciation

舺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 舟 (“boat”) + phonetic 甲 (kah)

艅.e: An extra 余 boat 舟; 余 also provides the pronunciation

艉.e: The back 尾 of a ship 舟; 尾 also provides the pronunciation

艏.e: The head 首 of a ship 舟; 首 also provides the pronunciation

艙.e: A ship's 舟 cabin 倉; 倉 also provides the pronunciation

艜.e: Vernacular reading of 舵 (Norman, 1988, Yan et al., 2016).

艢.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zaŋ): semantic 木 (“tree”) + phonetic 嗇 (OC *srɯɡ).

艮.e: Origin unclear. Originally written with 目 over 匕.

良.e: Pictograph of a hallway, now written as 廊. The character is based on the original meaning "hallway", which is unrelated to the modern meaning.

艳.e: Lush 丰 and sexy 色

艷.e: Lush 豐 and sexy 色

艹.e: Simplified form of 艸; grass sprouting

节.e: Simplified form of 節; sections of bamboo ⺮

芊.e: A thousand 千 plants 艹; 千 also provides the pronunciation

芐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡraːs, *ɡaːʔ): semantic 艸 (“grass; plant”) + phonetic 下 (OC *ɡraːʔ, *ɡraːs).

芟.e: To cut grass 艹 with a scythe 殳; 殳 also provides the pronunciation

芣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *bɯ): semantic 艸 (“grass; plant”) + phonetic 不 (OC *pɯ, *pɯʔ, *pɯ').

芯.e: The heart 心 of a plant 艹; 心 also provides the pronunciation

花.e: A flower 艹 in bloom 化; 化 also provides the pronunciation

芲.e: A flower 艹 in bloom 化; 化 also provides the pronunciation

芼.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *maːw, *maːws): semantic 艸 (“grass”) + phonetic 毛 (OC *maːw, *maːws)

苊.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 艹 (“grass; plant”) + phonetic 厄.

Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 𦰋 (“person”) + 戈 (“dagger-axe”) – a weapon striking a person's lower leg — to obliterate.
𦰋 (“person”) was composed of an eye with eyebrows (now written 眉) on top of 人. The eye and eyebrows are written ⿱⻀罒 in the modern form, and 人 has combined with 戈 to form 戍.

Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋon, *ŋonʔ): semantic 阝 + phonetic 元 (OC *ŋon).
; “ruan (musical instrument)”

阴.e: The place 阝 of the moon 月; compare 阳

陞.e: To pile up 升 earth 土 to form a mound 阝; 升 also provides the pronunciation

陣.e: A formation 阝of soldiers 車

陯.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *run): semantic 阜 (“mound”) + phonetic 侖 (OC *run)

隂.e: The shaded side of a hill 阝; 侌 provides the pronunciation

Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡ·ruːŋ): phonetic 降 (OC *kruːŋs, *ɡruːŋ) + semantic 生, with 𡕒 later dropped.
; "eminent; high"
; "thunder"

隣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 阝 + phonetic 粦 (OC *rins).

隬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 阝 (“mound”) + phonetic 爾 (OC *njelʔ).

隯.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 阝 + phonetic 壽 (OC *djuʔ, *djus).

隲.e: To climb 陟 on a horse 馬; 陟 also provides the pronunciation

雍.e: Birds 乡 living together in a village 乡

雒.e: From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *s-rik ~ s-ryak (“pheasant, partridge”); see 雉 (OC *l'iʔ, “pheasant”) for more (STEDT).

雖.e: This character originally was used to describe some sort of lizard-like animal. The current meaning is a phonetic loan.

雗.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡaːns): phonetic 倝 (OC *kaːns) + semantic 隹 (“pheasant”)

雜.e: Phonosemantic compound. 衣 represents the meaning and 集 represents the sound.

雠.e: Two birds 隹 exchanging angry words 讠; 雔 provides the pronunciation

雫.e:
Spelling pronunciation, as 下 (xià).
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 雫 (shizuku).

雰.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pʰɯn): semantic 雨 (“rain”) + phonetic 分 (OC *pɯn, *bɯns, “divide, small”).

雲.e: Rain 雨 falling from a cloud 云

雷.e: A storm 雨 over the fields 田

電.e: A thunderbolt 电 cast by a storm cloud 雨

霈.e: Rain 雨 causing a flood 沛; 沛 also provides the pronunciation

霍.e: Birds 隹 caught in a sudden storm 雨

霓.e: The child 兒 of a rain cloud 雨

霚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *moɡs, *moːŋ): semantic 雨 (“rain”) + phonetic 敄 (OC *moʔ, *mos).

霪.e: An obscene 淫 amount of rain 雨; 淫 also provides the pronunciation

霱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷid): semantic 雨 (“rain”) + phonetic 矞 (OC *ɢʷid).

霺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 雨 (“rain”) + phonetic 微 (OC *mɯl).

靄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qaːds, *qaːd): semantic 雨 (“rain”) + phonetic 謁 (OC *qad).

靈.e: Phonosemantic compound. 巫 represents the meaning and 霝 represents the sound.

靉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qɯːds, *qɯlʔ): semantic 雲 (“cloud”) + phonetic 愛 (OC *qɯːds)

青.e: Phonosemantic compound. 生 represents the meaning and 井 represents the sound.

靖.e: As peaceful 立 as nature 青; 青 also provides the pronunciation

靝.e: The heavens 一 above a man 大

非.e: Two bird's wings opposed; two people back-to-back

靧.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡluːds): semantic 面 + phonetic 貴 (OC *kluds).
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *hus ~ *h(u/i)r (“to wash; moisture; wet”); cognate with Mizo [Term?] (huh, “wet”), Tibetan ཧུས (hus, “moisture, humidity”) (STEDT).

靨.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qeb): phonetic 厭 (OC *qemʔ, *qems, *qeb) + semantic 面 (“face”).

靬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰaːn, *kʰaːns, *kreːn, *kan): semantic 革 (“leather”) + phonetic 干 (OC *kaːn).

靯.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'aːʔ): semantic 革 + phonetic 土 (OC *l̥ʰaːʔ, *l'aːʔ).

靹.e: Possibly the same word as 䪏 (MC nop).

鞄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *bruː, *bruːʔ, *bruːs, *pʰruːɡ): semantic 革 (“leather”) + phonetic 包 (OC *pruː).

鞏.e: Leather 革 bindings 巩; 巩 also provides the pronunciation

鞝.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 鞝 – see 緔 (“to sole a shoe”).
(This character is the simplified form of 緔).
 Notes:
Simplified Chinese is mainly used in Mainland China, Malaysia, and Singapore.
Traditional Chinese is mainly used in Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan.

鞣.e: To soften 柔 leather 革; 柔 also provides the pronunciation

鞤.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *paːŋ): phonetic 封 (OC *poŋ, *poŋs) + semantic 革.

鞧.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *skʰlu): semantic 革 (“leather”) + phonetic 酋 (OC *sɡlu)

鞪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mu, *moːɡ): phonetic 敄 (OC *moʔ, *mos) + semantic 革 (“leather”)

鞬.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kan): semantic 革 + phonetic 建 (OC *kans).

鞭.e: Phonosemantic compound. 革 represents the meaning and 便 represents the sound.

鞵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡreː): semantic 革 (“leather”) + phonetic 奚 (OC *ɡeː).

鞻.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *roː): semantic 革 (“leather”) + phonetic 婁 (OC *ɡ·roː, *ɡ·ro).

韇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'oːɡ): semantic 革 (“leather”) + phonetic 𧶠 (OC *luɡ)

韋.e: Pictograph of two feet walking around city walls. The character is based on the original meaning "to surround", which is unrelated to the modern meaning.

韓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡaːn): phonetic 倝 (OC *kaːns) + semantic 韋 (“surround”).

韔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tʰaŋs): semantic 韋 (“surround”) + phonetic 長 (OC *taŋʔ, *daŋ, *daŋs)

韞.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 韞 – see 困 (“to be surrounded; to be trapped”).
(This character is a variant form of 困).

韢.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *skʰʷeds): semantic 韋 + phonetic 惠 (OC *ɢʷiːds)

韣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *doːɡ, *tjoɡ, *djoɡ): semantic 韋 + phonetic 蜀 (OC *djoɡ)

韦.e: Simplified form of 韋.  Pictograph of two feet walking around city walls. The character is based on the original meaning "to surround", which is unrelated to the modern meaning.

韭.e: Chives 非 growing in the ground 一

韮.e: Chives 非 growing in the ground 一

音.e: A tongue 立 forming a sound in the mouth 日

韻.e: Of equal 員 tone 音; 員 also provides the pronunciation

頃.e:
Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 匕 + 頁 (“head”).
The head is askew, the original meaning of 傾／倾 (qīng).

頇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋaːns, *hŋaːn, *ŋaːd): phonetic 干 (OC *kaːn) + semantic 頁 (“leaf”)

頋.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 頋 – see 顧 (“to turn around and look; to look back; to look at; etc.”).
(This character is a variant form of 顧).

頒.e: To pass out 分 a booklet 頁; 分 also provides the pronunciation

頔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'ɯːwɢ): phonetic 由 (OC *lɯw) + semantic 頁.

頞.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qaːd): phonetic 安 (OC *qaːn) + semantic 頁 (“head”).
Possibly related to 鞍 (OC *qaːn, “saddle”) (Pulleyblank apud Goodrich, 1984).

頪.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 米 + 頁. Perhaps, according to an interpretation in the Shuowen Jiezi by Xu Shen, the first component is a contraction of 粉, which means that 頁 is the Kangxi radical.

頫.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l̥ʰeːws, *poʔ): phonetic 兆 (OC *l'ewʔ) + semantic 頁 (“head”).

頰.e: The jaw in 夾 the head 頁; 夾 also provides the pronunciation

頹.e: A bald 禿 head 頁; 禿 also provides the pronunciation

頻.e: Origin unclear. 步 (step) is possibly a meaning component alluding to frequent steps.

顑.e: STEDT compares it to Proto-Sino-Tibetan *k(w)am (“thirsty / dried out”), which is also linked to 歁 (OC *kʰluːmʔ, *kʰluːb), as well as Tibetan སྐམ་པོ (skam po).

顖.e: A skull from above, with 乂 marking the fontanelle

類.e: Depicts three different types of things that have different types, i.e. different types of rice (米), leaves (頁), and dogs (犬).

顥.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 景 (“daylight”) + 頁 (“head (hair)”). According to the Shuowen Jiezi Zhu, it represents bright sunlight and the white hair in someone's head, signifying whiteness.

顸.e: One with a dry 干 face 页; 干 also provides the pronunciation

颁.e: To pass out 分 a booklet 页; 分 also provides the pronunciation

颊.e: The jaw in 夹 the head 页; 夹 also provides the pronunciation

频.e: Simplified form of 頻.  Origin unclear. 步 (step) is possibly a meaning component alluding to frequent steps.

颓.e: A bald 秃 head 页; 秃 also provides the pronunciation

颟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *moːn): phonetic 㒼 (mán) + semantic 頁 (“leaf”)

颢.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 景 (“daylight”) + 頁 (“head (hair)”). According to the Shuowen Jiezi Zhu, it represents bright sunlight and the white hair in someone's head, signifying whiteness.

颣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ruːls): semantic 糸 + phonetic 頪 (OC *roːds).

颱.e: News 風 delivered from a platform 台

颳.e: The biting tongue 舌 of the wind 風

飆.e: Storm 猋 winds 風; 猋 also provides the pronunciation

飇.e: A pack of ferocious dogs 犬; a storm

飈.e: Storm 猋 winds 風; 猋 also provides the pronunciation

风.e: Simplified form of 風.  Originally written the same as 鳳 (phoenix) because the two characters sounded the same. Later the bottom part of the character was abbreviated to distinguish the two characters.

飠.e: A dish of food 良 with a lid on top 人

Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 食 (“food, rice”) + 人 (“person”) – a person eating rice, feeding.
Also Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声), with 食 acting as phonetic.

飱.e: An evening 夕 meal 食

餔.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *paː, *baːs): semantic 食 (“food”) + phonetic 甫 (OC *paʔ).

餕.e: Food 飠 left over 夋; 夋 also provides the pronunciation

餘.e: Extra 余 food 飠; 余 also provides the pronunciation

餡.e: Food 飠 stuffed in a hole 臽; 臽 also provides the pronunciation

館.e: A public 官 building where food 飠 is served; 官 also provides the pronunciation

餲.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qraːds, *qrads, *ɡaːd, *qaːd): semantic 食 (“food”) + phonetic 曷 (OC *ɡaːd).

餴.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pɯn): semantic 食 (“food”) + phonetic 奔 (OC *pɯːn, *pɯːns).

餸.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声) and ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): semantic 食 + phonetic 送 (sung3).
From 送 (sung³, “to accompany (a staple food)”) (Chan, 1998).

餹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'aːŋ): semantic 食 (“to eat”) + phonetic 唐 (OC *ɡl'aːŋ).

餽.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 餽 – see 饋 (“to send gift; to offer food to a superior; etc.”).
(This character is a variant traditional form of 饋).

餾.e: Distilled 留 wine 飠; 留 also provides the pronunciation

餿.e: Old 叟 food 飠; 叟 also provides the pronunciation

饋.e: Expensive 貴 food 飠; 貴 also provides the pronunciation

饐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qriɡs): semantic 食 (“food; to eat”) + phonetic 壹 (OC *qlid).

饙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pɯn): semantic 食 (“food”) + phonetic 賁 (OC *prals, *bɯl, *pɯːn, *bɯn)

饣.e: 饣 is a component form of 食.

饿.e: To want 我 food 饣; 我 also provides the pronunciation

馀.e: Extra 余 food 饣; 余 also provides the pronunciation

馆.e: A public 官 building where food 饣 is served; 官 also provides the pronunciation

馈.e: Expensive 贵 food 饣; 贵 also provides the pronunciation

馊.e: Old 叟 food 饣; 叟 also provides the pronunciation

馏.e: Distilled 留 wine 饣; 留 also provides the pronunciation

馑.e: A time of little 堇 food 饣; 堇 also provides the pronunciation

首.e: A deer's head 自 adorned with antlers 丷

馘.e: To cut off 或 the enemy's ears 首; 或 also provides the pronunciation

香.e: A herb 禾 with a sweet 甘 smell

馝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *bliːɡ, *bliɡ): semantic 香 (“fragrant”) + phonetic 必 (OC *pliɡ)

馦.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʰeːm): semantic 香 (“fragrance”) + phonetic 兼 (OC *keːm, *keːms).

馺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sqʰɯːb): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 及 (OC *ɡrɯb)

駄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 馬 + phonetic 太 (OC *tʰaːds).

駅.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 駅(えき) (eki).
Read as 驛／驿 (jik6) based on the etymology, as 站 (zaam6) using a semantic reading (訓讀 /训读 (xùndú)), or as 尺 (cek3) based on the apparent phonetic component (有邊讀邊 /有边读边 (yǒu biān dú biān)).

駎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'ɯwɢs): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 由 (OC *lɯw).

駟.e: Four 四 horses 馬; 四 also provides the pronunciation

駢.e: A team of horses 馬 working together 并

駦.e: A person 朕 riding a horse 馬

騇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hljaːʔ, *hljaːs): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 舍 (OC *hljaːʔ, *hljaːs).

騈.e: A team of horses 馬 working together 并

騐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 馬 + phonetic 念 (OC *nɯːms).

騖.e: A horse 馬 working hard 敄; 敄 also provides the pronunciation

騘.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰloːŋ): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 悤 (cōng).

騫.e: A soldier 宀 mounting a horse 马

騭.e: To climb 陟 on a horse 馬; 陟 also provides the pronunciation

騰.e: A person 朕 riding a horse 馬

騱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡeː): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 奚 (OC *ɡeː).

騷.e: A flea 蚤 biting a horse 馬; 蚤 also provides the pronunciation

騸.e: Compare perhaps Burmese သင်း (sang:, “to geld, castrate”).

騺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tibs): phonetic 執 (OC *tjib) + semantic 馬 (“horse”).

驁.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋaːw, *ŋaːws): phonetic 敖 (OC *ŋaːw) + semantic 馬 (“horse”).

驄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰloːŋ): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 悤 (cōng).

驍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋkeːw): semantic 馬 (“horse”) + phonetic 堯 (OC *ŋeːw).

驕.e: A proud 喬 horse 馬; 喬 also provides the pronunciation

驖.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l̥ʰiːɡ, *l'iːɡ): semantic 馬 + phonetic 𢧜 (zhì).
Perhaps related to 鐵 (OC *l̥ʰiːɡ, “iron”) as both may be derived from the etymon "black".

驜.e: Characters in the same phonetic series (業) (Zhengzhang, 2003)

马.e: Simplified form of 馬, a horse galloping to the left

驷.e: Four 四 horses 马; 四 also provides the pronunciation

骄.e: A proud 乔 horse 马; 乔 also provides the pronunciation

骈.e: A team of horses 马 working together 并

骘.e: To climb 陟 on a horse 马; 陟 also provides the pronunciation

骚.e: A flea 蚤 biting a horse 马; 蚤 also provides the pronunciation

骛.e: A horse 马 working hard 敄; 敄 also provides the pronunciation

骜.e: A rambling 敖 horse 马; 敖 also provides the pronunciation

骞.e: A soldier 宀 mounting a horse 马

骰.e: Tools 殳 made of bone 骨

骱.e: A bony 骨 joint 介; 介 also provides the pronunciation

骹.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰraːw): semantic 骨 (“bone”) + phonetic 交 (OC *kreːw).

骺.e: The end 后 of a bone 骨; 后 also provides the pronunciation

骽.e: Flesh ⺼ used to walk 退; 退 also provides the pronunciation

髀.e: A low 卑 bone 骨; 卑 also provides the pronunciation

髆.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *paːɡ): semantic 骨 (“bone”) + phonetic 尃 (OC *pʰa).
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *rV-pak (“shoulder”). Cognate with Tibetan ཕྲག་པ (phrag pa).

髇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʰraːw): semantic 骨 (“bone”) + phonetic 高 (OC *kaːw).

髝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *raːws): semantic 高 (“tall”) + phonetic 勞 (OC *raːw, *raːws).

髢.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'eːls): semantic 髟 (“long hair”) + phonetic 也 (OC *laːlʔ)

髺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *koːd): semantic 髟 (“appearance of long hair”) + phonetic 𠯑 (OC *ɡroːd)

鬄.e: To exchange 易 hair 髟; 易 also provides the pronunciation

鬇.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zraːŋ, *zreːŋ): semantic 髟 (“hair drooping”) + phonetic 爭 (OC *ʔsreːŋ).

鬈.e: Curled 卷 hair 髟; 卷 also provides the pronunciation

鬌.e: May be from Proto-Sino-Tibetan *(d/n)waj (“to hang from; to cling to; creeper”); see 垂 (OC *djol, “to hang down”) for more (STEDT).

鬙.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sɯːŋ): semantic 髟 (“hair”) + phonetic 曾 (OC *ʔsɯːŋ, *zɯːŋ).

鬟.e: A round 睘 topknot 髟; 睘 also provides the pronunciation

鬠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *koːbs, *ɡoːd): semantic 髟 (“appearance of long hair”) + phonetic 會 (OC *koːbs, *ɡoːbs)

鬡.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rneːŋ): semantic 髟 + phonetic 寧 (OC *neːŋ).

鬣.e: Whiskers 巤 of hair 髟; 巤 also provides the pronunciation

鬥.e: Two people 丨 boxing 王

鬦.e: Two people 丨 boxing 王

鬧.e: As busy as the market 市 gate 鬥

鬪.e: Two people 丨 boxing 王

鬬.e: Two people 丨 boxing 王

鬭.e: To fight 鬥 with an axe 斲; 鬥 also provides the pronunciation

鬮.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʷɯ, *kʷruʔ): semantic 鬥 (“to fight; to compete”) + phonetic 龜 (OC *kʷɯ, *kʷrɯ).

鬲.e: A cauldron 口 with three legs 冂丷

鬳.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋans): phonetic 虍 (OC *qʰaː) + semantic 鬲 (“cauldron”).

鬵.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zlam, *zɯm, *ljɯm): phonetic 兓 (OC *sl'ɯm) + semantic 鬲.
Possibly related to 甑 (OC *ʔsɯŋs) (Wang, 1982).

鬻.e: To sell porridge 粥 steaming in a pot 鬲

鬼.e: A ghost with a scary face

魁.e: One who fought 斗 a ghost 鬼; 鬼 also provides the pronunciation

魅.e: A forest 未 demon 鬼; 未 also provides the pronunciation

魋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *duːl): semantic 鬼 (“ghost”) + phonetic 隹 (OC *tjul).

魕.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 鬼 (“ghost”) + phonetic 幾 (OC *kɯl, *kɯlʔ, *ɡɯl, *ɡɯls).

魯.e: To talk 日 like a fish 魚

魵.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pʰɯnʔ, *pʰɯns, *bɯn, *bɯnʔ): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 分 (OC *pɯn, *bɯns).

鮀.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'aːl): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 它 (OC *l̥ʰaːl).

鮄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 弗 (OC *pɯd)

鮅.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pliɡ, *bliɡ): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 必 (bì)

鮋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'ɯw, *ɦljɯw, *lɯw): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 由 (OC *lɯw).

鮎.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *neːm): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 占 (OC *ʔljem, *tjems).

鮟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 安 (OC *qaːn).

鮣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qiŋs): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 印 (OC *qiŋs).

鮸.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mronʔ): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 免 (OC *mronʔ).

鯓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 身.

鯱.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 鯱 (shachi).
Spelling pronunciation, as 虎 (hǔ).

鰇.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mju): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 柔 (OC *mlju).
Specialization of 柔 (MC nyuw, “soft; flexible”).

鰉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 皇 (OC *ɡʷaːŋ).

鰍.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sʰɯw): semantic 魚 + phonetic 秋 (OC *sʰɯw).

鰏.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 畐 (OC *pʰrɯɡ, *bɯɡ).

鰨.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tʰaːb): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 𦐇 (tà)

鰯.e:
Orthographic borrowing from Japanese 鰯 (iwashi, “sardine”). A Japanese kokuji coined phono-semanically (弱し yowashi) in the Nara period.
Spelling pronunciation, as 弱 (ruò)

鰷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'ɯːw): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 條 (OC *l'ɯːw).

鰺.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 鰺 – see 䱗 (“sharpbelly”).
(This character is a variant form of 䱗).

鱑.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 皇 (OC *ɡʷaːŋ).

鱝.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *bɯnʔ): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 賁 (OC *prals, *bɯl, *pɯːn, *bɯn).

鱟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡoːɡs, *quːɡ): abbreviated phonetic 學 (OC *ɡruːɡ) + semantic 魚 (“fish”)

鱵.e: Attested in Shan Hai Jing as 箴 (“needle”) (Zhengzitong).

鱻.e: A fresh catch of fish 魚

鲁.e: To talk 日 like a fish 鱼

鲉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'ɯw, *ɦljɯw, *lɯw): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 由 (OC *lɯw).

鲹.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 鰺 – see 䱗 (“sharpbelly”).
(This character is a variant form of 䱗).

鲾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 魚 (“fish”) + phonetic 畐 (OC *pʰrɯɡ, *bɯɡ).

鳦.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qriːd, *qrid): semantic 鳥 (“bird”) + phonetic 𠃉.
Related to 燕 (OC *qeːns, “swallow”) (Wang, 1982). See there for more.

鳪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *poːɡ): phonetic 卜 (OC *poːɡ) + semantic 鳥.

鳮.e: Another 奚 kind of bird 隹

鳴.e: The sound 口 a bird 鳥 makes

鳽.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kŋeːn, *ŋeːn, *ŋ̊ʰreːŋ, *ŋreːɡ): phonetic 幵 (OC *kŋeːn) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鴠.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *taːns): phonetic 旦 (OC *taːns) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鴳.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qraːns): phonetic 安 (OC *qaːn) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *ha:r (“fowl, chicken”) (STEDT).

鴴.e: A wading 行 bird 鳥; 鳥 also provides the pronunciation

鴽.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *nja): phonetic 如 (OC *nja, *njas) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鵄.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tʰji): phonetic 至 (OC *tjiɡs) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”)

鵋.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡɯs): phonetic 忌 (OC *ɡɯs) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鵱.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ruɡ): semantic 坴 + phonetic 鳥 (OC *tɯːwʔ, “bird”)

鶂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋeːɡ): phonetic 兒 (OC *ŋeː, *ŋje) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鶆.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rɯː): phonetic 來 (OC *m·rɯːɡ) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鶒.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tʰɯɡ): phonetic 勅 (OC *tʰɯɡ) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鶯.e: A bird 鳥 covered in 冖 green 炏 feathers

鶲.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 翁 (OC *qloːŋ) + semantic 鳥

鶳.e: Combined character of 獅鳥 /狮鸟 (si¹ niu⁵), which is alternative form of 私了 (si¹ liu⁵) on the Internet.

鶶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *l'aːŋ): phonetic 唐 (OC *ɡl'aːŋ) + phonetic 鳥 (OC *tɯːwʔ, “bird”).

鶾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡaːn): phonetic 倝 (OC *kaːns) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”)

鶿.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsɯ, *zɯ): phonetic 玆 (OC *ɡʷeːn) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”)

鷁.e: Probably from Proto-Sino-Tibetan *s-ŋak (“bird”), and cognate with 鶚 (OC *ŋaːɡ, “osprey”) (STEDT) and Burmese ငှက် (hngak, “id”) (Hill 2019). Said Sino-Tibetan root may be an areal word; compare Proto-Austronesian *manuk (“bird, chicken”).

鷃.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qraːns): phonetic 安 (OC *qaːn) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *ha:r (“fowl, chicken”) (STEDT).

鷇.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kʰoːɡs): semantic 鳥 + phonetic 𣪊.
Cognate with 穀 (OC *kloːɡ, “baby”) and 㝅 (“to suckle”) (Schuessler, 2007).

鷉.e: An amphibious 虒 bird 虒; 虒 also provides the pronunciation

鷕.e: Originally written as 隹; later written 唯 with 口 added. Now phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷiʔ, *ɢʷiʔ): phonetic 唯 (OC *ɢʷi, *ɢʷiʔ) + semantic 鳥.

鷞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sraŋ, *sraŋʔ): phonetic 爽 (OC *sraŋʔ) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”)

鷥.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *slɯ): phonetic 絲 (OC *slɯ) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”)

鷭.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ban): phonetic 番 (OC *paːl, *paːls, *pʰaːn, *pʰan, *ban) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鷮.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *krew, *ɡrew): phonetic 喬 (OC *krew, *ɡrew) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).
Sagart (2017) relates it to 喬 (OC *krew, *ɡrew, “tall; lofty”), Tibetan སྒྲོ (sgro, “to elevate; large feather”), Jingpho shagrau (“to exalt”).

鷷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ʔsun, *zun): phonetic 尊 (OC *ʔsuːn) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”)

鸂.e: Water 氵 fowl 鷄; 鷄 also provides the pronunciation

鸎.e: A bird 鳥 covered in 冖 green 炏 feathers

鸐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *r'eːwɢ): phonetic 翟 (OC *r'aːwɢ, *l'eːwɢ) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鸒.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *la, *las): phonetic 與 (OC *la, *laʔ, *las) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *ka-n (“crow”). Cognate with 烏 (OC *qaː) and 鴉 (OC *qraː), both meaning "crow, raven".

鸓.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rul, *rulʔ): phonetic 畾 (OC *ruːl) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”).

鸛.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *koːns): phonetic 雚 (OC *koːns, “stork”) + semantic 鳥 (“bird”). Originally written 雚.

鸟.e: Simplified form of 烏, a bird

鸡.e: Another 又 kind of bird 鸟

鸣.e: The sound 口 a bird 鸟 makes

鸻.e: A wading 行 bird 鸟; 鸟 also provides the pronunciation

鹟.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 翁 (OC *qloːŋ) + semantic 鳥

鹵.e: A container used to brine pickles

鹺.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zlaːl): semantic 鹵 (“salt”) + phonetic 差 (OC *sʰraːl, *sʰraːls, *sʰreːl, *sʰreːl, *sʰreːls, *sʰral).
From Proto-Sino-Tibetan *tsa (“salt”) (STEDT). Cognate with Tibetan ཚྭ (tshwa), Situ tsʰe, Burmese ဆား (hca:) and Nuosu ꋂ (ce).

鹿.e: A deer with an antlered head, two legs, and a tail

麀.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 鹿 (“deer”) + abbreviated 牝 (“female”).

麃.e: Tracks 灬 left by a deer 鹿

麑.e: Possibly related to 倪 (OC *ŋeː, “small and weak”) (Schuessler, 2007).

麗.e: The antlers of a deer 鹿

麚.e: Wang (1982) relates this word to 豭 (OC *kraː, “pig”), 羖 (OC *klaːʔ, “(black) ram”), and 牯 (OC *kaːʔ, “male bovine”). See 豭 (jiā) for more.

麥.e: Grains 來 ready to be harvested 夂; compare 麦

麦.e: Simplified form of 麦; grains 來 ready to be harvested 夂

麪.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *meːns): semantic 麥 (“wheat”) + phonetic 丏 (OC *meːnʔ).

麭.e: Borrowed from Japanese パン (pan, “bread”), from Portuguese pão (“bread”), from Latin panem, accusative singular form of panis (“bread”).

麰.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *mu): semantic 麥 (“wheat”) + phonetic 牟 (OC *mu).
Perhaps from Proto-Sino-Tibetan *(m/b)rəw (“grain; seed; lineage”) (STEDT); see 物 (OC *mɯd, “thing, matter, substance”) for more cognates.

麷.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *pʰuŋ): semantic 麥 (“wheat”) + phonetic 豐 (OC *pʰuŋ).

麻.e: Hemp plants 林 growing in a greenhouse 广

麽.e: Phonosemantic compound. 么 represents the meaning and 麻 represents the sound. Variant form of 麼.

黍.e: Grain 禾 that can be fermented to make wine 氺

黐.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *rel, *r̥ʰel): semantic 黍 (“glutinous millet”) + phonetic 离 (OC *rel, *r̥ʰel).

黑.e: A man's face blacked by soot from a fire 灬

默.e: A dog 犬 watching in the dark 黑

黙.e: A dog 犬 watching in the dark 黑

黚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɡram, *ɡrɯm): semantic 黑 (“black”) + phonetic 甘 (OC *kaːm) – to assess; to examine.

黮.e: From Kra-Dai. Compare Proto-Tai *C̥.damᴬ (“black”) (whence Thai ดำ (dam, “black”)) (Schuessler, 2003). See also 黔 and 紺.

黴.e: A small 微 black 黑 growth; 微 also provides the pronunciation

黶.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qemʔ): phonetic 厭 (OC *qemʔ, *qems, *qeb) + semantic 黑 (“black”).

黸.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *raː): semantic 黑 (“black”) + phonetic 盧 (OC *b·raː) – black.

黾.e: Simplified form of 黽; a frog with bug-eyes

鼃.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ɢʷreː, *qʷreː): phonetic 圭 (OC *kʷeː) + semantic 黽 (“frog”).

鼈.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 敝 (OC *beds, *bed) + semantic 黽.

鼎.e: A cauldron on its bronze tripod

鼏.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *meːɡ): phonetic 冖 (OC *meːɡ, “cover”) + semantic 鼎 (“ding”)

鼒.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zlɯː, *ʔslɯ): phonetic 才 (OC *zlɯː, *zlɯːs) + semantic 鼎.

鼘.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qrin, *qʷiːn): semantic 鼓 (“drum”) + phonetic 淵 (OC *qʷiːn).

鼚.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tʰaŋ): semantic 鼓 (“drums”) + phonetic 長 (OC *taŋʔ, *daŋ, *daŋs)

鼠.e: A mouse, with two paws to the left and the tail to the right

鼣.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意): 鼠 (“mouse; rat”) + 犬 (“dog”).

齂.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *hmrɯːds, *hmrɯds, *mrɯːd): semantic 鼻 (“nose”) + phonetic 隶 (OC *lɯds).

齅.e: Ideogrammic compound (會意 /会意) and phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *qʰlus): semantic 鼻 (“nose”) + phonetic 臭 (OC *kʰljus, “smell”).

齉.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 鼻 + phonetic 囊 (OC *naːŋ).

齊.e: Three stalks of grain growing evenly

齐.e: Simplified form of 齊.  Pictograph of stalks of millet. Possibly alludes to the even and uniform appearance of a millet field.

齒.e: Upper and lower teeth 从; 止 provides the pronunciation

齞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋleːnʔ): semantic 齒 (“tooth”) + phonetic 只 (OC *klje, *kljeʔ).

齣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 齒 (“tooth”) + phonetic 句 (OC *koː, *koːs, *kos, *ɡo).

齩.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋɡreːwʔ): semantic 齒 (“tooth”) + phonetic 交 (OC *kreːw).

齭.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *skʰraʔ, *sqʰraʔ): semantic 齒 (“tooth”) + phonetic 所 (OC *sqʰraʔ).

齺.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *zroːɡ): semantic 齒 (“tooth”) + phonetic 芻 (OC *sʰro).

齻.e:
Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *tiːn): semantic 齒 + phonetic 真 (OC *ʔljin).
Related to Tibetan མཆེ་བ (mche ba, “fang, tusk”).

齼.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *sŋ̊ʰraʔ): semantic 齒 (“tooth”) + phonetic 楚 (OC *sŋ̊ʰraʔ, *sŋ̊ʰras).

齾.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *ŋaːd, *ŋraːd): phonetic 獻 (OC *sŋaːl, *hŋans, *ŋrad) + semantic 齒 (“tooth”).

齿.e: Teeth 人凵; 止 provides the pronunciation

龍.e: A dragon facing left and bending downwards

龎.e: A dragon 龍 inside a house 广

龐.e: A dragon 龍 inside a house 广

龑.e: Liu Yan's original name was 劉巖 /刘岩 (liú yán). He changed 巖 /岩 (yán) to 龑, a character he invented. The character refers to a saying in the I Ching, 飛龍在天 ("the flying dragon in the sky").

龒.e: A dragon facing left and bending downwards

龜.e: A turtle on its side with its shell to the right

龝.e: The time of year when farmers would burn 禾 crops 火

龞.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): phonetic 敝 (OC *beds, *bed) + semantic 龜

龣.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声, OC *kroːɡ, *roːɡ): semantic 龠 + phonetic 彔 (OC *b·roːɡ).

龰.e: 龰 is a component form of 止.

鿃.e: Phono-semantic compound (形聲 /形声): semantic 目 + phonetic 㚒.

鿏.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 䥑 – see 錽 (“a kind of decoration on a horse's head”).
(This character is a variant form of 錽).
Borrowed from English meitnerium, via Mandarin.

鿫.e: Coined in 2017 by the Chinese National Committee for Terms in Sciences and Technologies and State Language Commission.

鿬.e: Coined in 2017 by the Chinese National Committee for Terms in Sciences and Technologies and State Language Commission.

鿭.e:
For pronunciation and definitions of 鉨 – see 璽 (“imperial seal; royal seal”).
(This character is a variant form of 璽).
Coined in 2017 by the Chinese National Committee for Terms in Sciences and Technologies and State Language Commission.
